name: With Honor Action
subject: Here's 5 Things You Probably Missed in the NDAA
body:
Advancing American biotechnology, countering the CCP and Russia, streamlining DoD's weapons purchasing process, and more.

| | | | | [|
---](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=ad3acfe20a&e=33f53a5b1a)
| | | | [|
---](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=495f657772&e=33f53a5b1a)
| | _The original NDAA was half of a page and authorized spending for three programs: Aircrafts, Missiles, and Naval Vessels. This year’s NDAA is 3,086 pages._
---
| | Hi Peter,
Congress just did something rare: It was consistent. For the 65th year in a row, Congress passed the National Defense Authorization Act (known as the “NDAA”) this week on a broad, bipartisan basis.
The first NDAA was half of a page—you can read the entire thing above. [This year’s NDAA is a whopping 3,086 pages](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=40a2e8e2c7&e=33f53a5b1a), a big bill that sets policy for the entire Department of Defense, authorizing and allocating $900.6 billion, including a 3.8 percent pay raise for service members. Congress also included authorizations for the Department of State, the Coast Guard, intelligence agencies, and parts of the Department of Energy in this year’s bill.
It’s also an opportunity for organizations like [With Honor Action to weigh in](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4d8b78e38f&e=33f53a5b1a) on a consistent, bipartisan, bicameral process on policies that affect all Americans and our place in the world. So while you’re reading the entire bill during this holiday season, here are five takeaways from our work on this year’s NDAA.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=da12b1c3c4&e=33f53a5b1a)
| | |
# 1\. DoD Acquisition Reform

A core priority of With Honor Action ally Senator Roger Wicker, the NDAA delivers results by reforming how the DoD buys weapons by cutting red tape, accelerating acquisition, scaling proven technologies faster, and overall, requiring DoD to determine if products or services can be obtained easier commercially. These reforms get new capabilities out of the lab, or off of shelves, and into the hands of warfighters faster.
---
| |
# Spotlight: Sec. 1822 - _Modifications to Commercial Products and Commercial Services_ a.k.a the [FoRGED Act](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c1214eaf75&e=33f53a5b1a)

Led by With Honor Action ally and Senate Armed Services Committee Chairman Senator Roger Wicker.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |
# 2\. Boosts to Youth Service Programs

The NDAA invests tangible resources in civic youth programs by adding 200 more [JROTC](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=a9f1fd0232&e=33f53a5b1a) units and authorizing $4.3 million for the [U.S. Naval Sea Cadet Corps](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c83e397d28&e=33f53a5b1a), $50 million for the [National Guard STARBASE](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f3ed102512&e=33f53a5b1a) program, and $50.3 million for the [Civil Air Patrol](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=019c852324&e=33f53a5b1a). For Country Caucus member Rep. Salud Carbajal is a [Co-Chair of the Congressional STARBASE Caucus](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=18c431fa55&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
| |
---
| |
# Spotlight: Sec. 542 - _Number of Junior Reserve Officers’ Training Corps Units_
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=2852e3dc6c&e=33f53a5b1a)
| | |
# 3\. Congress Goes Big on Biotech

This year’s NDAA includes [17 provisions](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=052398dd34&e=33f53a5b1a) to protect and advance U.S. leadership in biotechnology, including the creation of a DoD biotech coordination office and restrictions on federal agencies contracting with CCP-linked biotech firms. Many of these provisions were led by [NSCEB](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=5e6d7f62c8&e=33f53a5b1a) Chair and With Honor Action ally Senator Todd Young.
---
| |
# Spotlight: Section 851 - _Prohibition on contracting with certain biotechnology providers_ a.k.a. the [BIOSECURE Act](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c63f704b45&e=33f53a5b1a)

Led by With Honor Action ally and Ranking Member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee Senator Gary Peters, a Navy veteran, and previously by former Representatives and For Country Caucus members Brad Wenstrup, an Army veteran, and Mike Gallagher, a Marine Corps veteran.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | | |
# 4\. Prioritizing Warfighter Health

Building on our work [last year](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=c2b25a189a&e=33f53a5b1a) on TBIs and service member brain health, this year’s NDAA establishes blast safety officer positions and requires a study on the high rates of cancer among military rotary-wing pilots and aircrew, strengthening the DoD’s oversight and understanding of the hidden costs of war.
---
| [|
---](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=76e885bdb8&e=33f53a5b1a)
| |
# Spotlight: Sec. 736 - _Study on prevalence and mortality of cancer among military rotary-wing pilots and aviation support personnel_ a.k.a. the [ROTOR Act](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4b9ae25d9d&e=33f53a5b1a)

Led by For Country Caucus member Rep. Rich McCormick, a Marine Corps and Navy veteran.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=66cb6d7b1d&e=33f53a5b1a)
| | |
# 5\. Support for Our Allies & Partners

Congress delivers support for allies on the front lines, doubling down on Ukraine’s fight to protect its sovereignty, reinforcing deterrence in the Baltics, and strengthening Taiwan’s defense against coercion. Notably, the NDAA reauthorizes and increases by $100 million the Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative, which helps build and maintain Ukraine’s capability to defend itself by buying equipment, weapons, and training from U.S. sources.
---
| |
# Spotlight: Section 1247 - _Baltic Security Initiative_ a.k.a. the [Baltic Security Assessment Act of 2025](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=cd16eb9b83&e=33f53a5b1a)

Led by Founding For Country Caucus Co-Chair Rep. Don Bacon, an Air Force veteran, and former Vice Chair Rep. Salud Carbajal, a Marine Corps veteran.
---
|
---
| | In case you’re not ready to read the 3,087-page bill yet, you can catch With Honor’s roll-up of our priorities that passed in this year’s NDAA below.
---
| [With Honor Announces 70 Priorities Passed in the NDAA](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=4f40419c40&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | Thank you for standing with us,The With Honor Action Team
---
| |
# Follow us on social media
---
| | | | | [](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=8ed208454f&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
[](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=76ce6307be&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
[](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=775415b571&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
[](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=d6465d6a23&e=33f53a5b1a)

We work alongside the bipartisan For Country Caucus in Congress to pass critical legislation in national security, national service, and veterans' affairs. With Honor has an affiliated political action committee called With Honor Fund III.

I served this country as an Army Ranger in Iraq and Afghanistan. I took an oath to defend the Constitution, and I still live by that oath today in Congress.
Now, the President of the United States, Donald Trump, is calling for me and other veterans to be imprisoned and murdered:

name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Children need to know
body:
Sadly, most Americans – and especially our children – have come to believe that the rainbow is a symbol of the LGBTQ+ movement.

| | |
---
| |
---
Dear Friend,If you agree that it’s time for Christians to boldly and unashamedly lead our schools and our culture back to our country’s founding values......will you help me teach children the true meaning of the rainbow?The Bible tells us that God gave us the rainbow as a symbol of his covenant with the earth:
---
**“I have set my rainbow in the clouds, and it will be the sign of the covenant between me and the earth.” Genesis 9:13**
---
Sadly, most Americans – and especially our children – have come to believe that the rainbow is a symbol of the LGBTQ+ movement.After all, it was back in 1999 that Bill Clinton first recognized June as “LGBTQ+ Pride Month.” And LGBTQ+ “rainbow” flags now fly year-round in stores, libraries, schools, and the halls of Congress...and even in some churches. **[That’s why I’m hoping you will take just one simple action today to help me teach children the true meaning of the rainbow and God’s word.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z3hi4m7bu6an009dvdk8ftr8mn16t61dgrci11ko_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs)** With Christianity under attack in America, our nation desperately needs Christians who are willing to stand up and defend our faith.My name is Kirk Cameron.Maybe you recognize me from the 1980’s family sitcom “Growing Pains” or from my work in inspirational movies like “Fireproof” and “Monumental.”I know firsthand how dangerous it is to be indoctrinated by leftist values. Pride almost took me down as a young actor in Hollywood.But the gospel saved me.I love being an actor, but what’s far more important is being a husband, dad, grandfather, and a Christian.When I saw the anti-God, anti-family, and anti-America messages the Left was pushing on young parents and children, I knew I had a duty to take action. So together with a small Christian book publisher aptly named **BRAVE Books** , I wrote “As You Grow” to teach children how to grow in wisdom through the fruit of the spirit.But that’s not all they are doing.BRAVE Books is giving parents, grandparents, and teachers the necessary tools to combat the Left’s hijacking of our children’s hearts and minds in schools, libraries, and elsewhere.And now, because of faithful Americans like you, God’s Word is being heard in the public square once again.Friend, I’m used to receiving hate mail from atheists and radical Left-wing activists who don’t like our faith message.But their responses make me realize just how much the LGBTQ+ community has changed our culture in just one generation.In the past, the gay community largely claimed they just wanted “tolerance.”But now that’s not enough.Today, they want everyone to celebrate and approve of their lifestyle – and they’re accusing those who don’t of “hate speech.” But by not standing up for God’s Word, Christians have allowed the Left’s indoctrination to creep into our churches, schools, and into our nation.And now we’re seeing the terrible effects of our inaction: our young children are being corrupted and led astray from the Faith. Think of all the drag queen story hours being held in public libraries, or the soft porn being promoted as teen reading, or even the classrooms where leftist teachers are sharing their “sexual orientation” and pronouns with kids as young as 5-years-old.More and more people are calling good evil...and evil good (Isaiah 5:20). My heart is heavy when I think of what’s in store for this nation if we don’t protect our children from these demonic influences.Never before has our culture so desperately needed parents and teachers to point our children towards the truth and away from the lies of man.
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zd47un3hdqftn3v3dpdljsg7ishbusm088losog0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs)
---
| That’s exactly why **BRAVE Books** started the “See You at the Library” movement and my children’s TV show “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” -- to boldly, publicly and unashamedly remind Christians and non-believers alike of the truth of God’s Word and the Christian values our country was founded on.
---
| | That’s exactly why **BRAVE Books** started the “See You at the Library” movement and my children’s TV show “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” -- to boldly, publicly and unashamedly remind Christians and non-believers alike of the truth of God’s Word and the Christian values our country was founded on.
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z9bp4vssmuu9eviuhv532kc5qm61n02kmgi3f2v0_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs)
---
| | Our national “See You at the Library” movement has reached tens of thousands of children in thousands of libraries across the country – including the Library of Congress!At each event, we pray together, say the pledge of allegiance, and read books that instill Godly and patriotic values in our kids.
---
| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zlje505l0jdfrq581e0k17ofo8qlr6qn656jc3mg_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs)
---
| | Our national “See You at the Library” movement has reached tens of thousands of children in thousands of libraries across the country – including the Library of Congress!At each event, we pray together, say the pledge of allegiance, and read books that instill Godly and patriotic values in our kids.
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zcmojtck10dvmqhjj8sot6erdc015qsar5fhophg_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs)
---
| | Just the thing we need to counteract the evil “Drag Queen Story Hours” and every other leftist lie.And with our TV show “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” we are reaching hundreds of thousands of families with timeless Christian values.Giving parents and kids an alternative to the filth being shown on screens today. As you can imagine, secularists despise our mission. They do not want us engaging the culture with biblical truths. And they are doing everything in their power to discredit, shame, and silence us. If you agree that we must introduce thousands of more children to the truth of God’s Word in these troubled times, [**then I would like to invite you to be a part of our life-changing mission here at BRAVE Books.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zrm6q2j10pdobqsfespjm81pf7h8luuet013rmq8_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs) Will you help us teach children Christian virtues and proclaim truth to a hurting world that needs God’s Word more than ever?So many children simply don’t know the truth about God or His love for them. That’s why I’m hoping you’ll help us reach even more families through **BRAVE Books** ’ crucial projects, like our Christmas “Mouse in the House,” a Godly alternative to that secular “elf” game that so many families use, or producing the next season of “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” giving children wholesome content that entertains while building their character. [**Would you prayerfully consider making a gift to BRAVE Books today?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zkuvojvsjr5skglegk4mi33fi36df8lk3lgbgmho_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs) America’s public schools are “normalizing” the ungodly agenda of the radical Left by decorating classrooms and hallways with “Gay Pride” rainbows, filling library shelves with LGBTQ+ books, and pushing movies and TV shows that glorify sinful behavior.They’re leading our children away from Christ...and toward an empty, godless and hopeless secular doctrine. But **BRAVE Books** is giving parents and grandparents a way to fight back and teach the next generation that God’s word is GOOD. Thank you for supporting BRAVE Books – and for helping us protect our precious children and grandchildren!
---
| | Sincerely,
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4173fbfar2cpgvvc65q420gh1nbbufekqpgbaio_rp22sh2s8i6eor3chhm6pj2c5h2bs)
---
Kirk Cameron

Think of all the drag queen story hours being held in public libraries, or the soft porn being promoted as teen reading, or even the classrooms where leftist teachers are sharing their “sexual orientation” and pronouns with kids as young as 5-years-old.More and more people are calling good evil...and evil good (Isaiah 5:20). My heart is heavy when I think of what’s in store for this nation if we don’t protect our children from these demonic influences.Never before has our culture so desperately needed parents and teachers to point our children towards the truth and away from the lies of man. | | | That’s exactly why BRAVE Books started the “See You at the Library” movement and my children’s TV show “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” -- to boldly, publicly and unashamedly remind Christians and non-believers alike of the truth of God’s Word and the Christian values our country was founded on. | | That’s exactly why BRAVE Books started the “See You at the Library” movement and my children’s TV show “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” -- to boldly, publicly and unashamedly remind Christians and non-believers alike of the truth of God’s Word and the Christian values our country was founded on. | | Our national “See You at the Library” movement has reached tens of thousands of children in thousands of libraries across the country – including the Library of Congress!At each event, we pray together, say the pledge of allegiance, and read books that instill Godly and patriotic values in our kids. | | | Our national “See You at the Library” movement has reached tens of thousands of children in thousands of libraries across the country – including the Library of Congress!At each event, we pray together, say the pledge of allegiance, and read books that instill Godly and patriotic values in our kids. | | Just the thing we need to counteract the evil “Drag Queen Story Hours” and every other leftist lie.And with our TV show “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” we are reaching hundreds of thousands of families with timeless Christian values.Giving parents and kids an alternative to the filth being shown on screens today. As you can imagine, secularists despise our mission. They do not want us engaging the culture with biblical truths. And they are doing everything in their power to discredit, shame, and silence us. If you agree that we must introduce thousands of more children to the truth of God’s Word in these troubled times, Will you help us teach children Christian virtues and proclaim truth to a hurting world that needs God’s Word more than ever?So many children simply don’t know the truth about God or His love for them. That’s why I’m hoping you’ll help us reach even more families through BRAVE Books ’ crucial projects, like our Christmas “Mouse in the House,” a Godly alternative to that secular “elf” game that so many families use, or producing the next season of “Iggy and Mr. Kirk” giving children wholesome content that entertains while building their character. America’s public schools are “normalizing” the ungodly agenda of the radical Left by decorating classrooms and hallways with “Gay Pride” rainbows, filling library shelves with LGBTQ+ books, and pushing movies and TV shows that glorify sinful behavior.They’re leading our children away from Christ...and toward an empty, godless and hopeless secular doctrine. But BRAVE Books is giving parents and grandparents a way to fight back and teach the next generation that God’s word is GOOD. Thank you for supporting BRAVE Books – and for helping us protect our precious children and grandchildren! | | Sincerely, Kirk Cameron | | | Brave Books 3528 S Loop 336 E Suite CConroe, TX 77301 | Pop Acta Media | American Action News PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

| **__A Personal Note from Elise Stefanik_ _**
---
Peter,
---
Our chances of beating Kathy Hochul depend on hitting _ALL_ of our fundraising benchmarks between now and Election Day.
---
But as it currently stands, we’re in danger of falling behind just days before our most important benchmark yet...
---
**_...our _FIRST_ and _ONLY_ end-of-year deadline!_**

Unless we raise another **_$19,055_** before tonight’s mid-month cutoff, we’ll officially enter the final stretch of 2025 behind budget!

With the holidays coming up, this would be the worst possible time to fall behind.

**To escape this looming crisis, I’m turning to my TOP grassroots supporters like _YOU_ for last-minute help reaching this vital goal. **

clean_body: We’re in danger of falling behind... | A Personal Note from Elise Stefanik Peter, Our chances of beating Kathy Hochul depend on hitting ALL of our fundraising benchmarks between now and Election Day. But as it currently stands, we’re in danger of falling behind just days before our most important benchmark yet... ...our FIRST and ONLY end-of-year deadline! Unless we raise another $19,055 before tonight’s mid-month cutoff, we’ll officially enter the final stretch of 2025 behind budget! With the holidays coming up, this would be the worst possible time to fall behind. To escape this looming crisis, I’m turning to my TOP grassroots supporters like YOU for last-minute help reaching this vital goal. As always: If you’ve given all that you can, or can’t afford to contribute right now, please simply disregard what I’m about to ask... But at this late hour, you’re truly my last hope, Peter. Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

Dear USO Supporter,
We're just one week away from Christmas. Gifts are being wrapped, to-do lists are being checked off and families everywhere are preparing for the warmth of the season.
Yet hundreds of thousands of our heroes in uniform won't be home to enjoy the magic of Christmas morning with those they love. Instead, they'll be in remote locations, putting their lives on the line in defense of our nation and sacrificing time with loved ones.
**" To get a care package is hope," says one U.S. Air Force veteran. "When I used to get USO Care Packages when I was deployed, it brought us happiness; it showed us people cared." **
**[This year, help make sure our heroes know they aren't forgotten, from holiday care packages and Christmas meals to Wi-Fi for phone calls home. Your generous donation today will help support our troops this Christmas and be matched up to $100,000, thanks to FedEx. ](https://links.e.uso.)**
That means your generosity goes twice as far to provide our troops with what they need most.
**[$29 CAN HELP PROVIDE A PHONE CALL HOME](https://links.e.uso.)**
---
**[$75 CAN HELP PROVIDE A HOLIDAY MEAL](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/2dpGh-2zWxMN/anBsdmsybk83cGdTRXpLU1RGRDdSbjNtYjBvYVBGZVFFdXhUMDBYRkU4eHcwR01xVGkvZDZBd1EzRlBDRElOTEtBUVFuZVVSUWtEUHltbnBtTUZLaUdudkRCYnZUdzFCWVFSQzUzOC8xZzQ9S0/d2Y2dFZTSVIwM1RUUmRXeUF0OU5jUXBxVVZnaHN4UjhRemJYUGtzMHJzMVhkM2p6dEhxSDgyNGI4K0RuRWxIOUswemlKLzFyeVlMaGpEZ2R0VGtiZDBGUjRqVENMbVBSS0)**
---
**[$115 CAN HELP PROVIDE A HOLIDAY GIFT BUNDLE](https://links.e.uso.)**
---
**[I WANT TO GIVE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://links.e.uso.)**
---
**These gifts aren 't items that are nice to have -- they're the everyday necessities we don't think twice about but mean the world to our service members. They're vital reminders that we haven't forgotten those who bravely serve our country.**
**[Please make your tax-deductible gift now and help our service members feel supported and remembered this Christmas season.](https://links.e.uso.)**
For the People Who Serve,
The USO
FedEx is matching all gifts to the USO up to a total of $100,000, through December 31, 2025. Your contribution will be used where it is needed most.

clean_body: The clock is ticking. Your gift goes 2X as far to directly help our troops. Dear USO Supporter, We're just one week away from Christmas. Gifts are being wrapped, to-do lists are being checked off and families everywhere are preparing for the warmth of the season. Yet hundreds of thousands of our heroes in uniform won't be home to enjoy the magic of Christmas morning with those they love. Instead, they'll be in remote locations, putting their lives on the line in defense of our nation and sacrificing time with loved ones. " To get a care package is hope," says one U.S. Air Force veteran. "When I used to get USO Care Packages when I was deployed, it brought us happiness; it showed us people cared." That means your generosity goes twice as far to provide our troops with what they need most. These gifts aren 't items that are nice to have -- they're the everyday necessities we don't think twice about but mean the world to our service members. They're vital reminders that we haven't forgotten those who bravely serve our country. For the People Who Serve, The USO FedEx is matching all gifts to the USO up to a total of $100,000, through December 31, 2025. Your contribution will be used where it is needed most. United Service Organizations, Inc. (USO) is a nonprofit 501(ς)(3) organization (EIN/Tax ID: 13-1610451) and not part of the Department of Defense (DOD). name: Jon Ossoff for Georgia
subject: Absolutely pummeled
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
Jon needs our help today, Peter
#

Peter,
As you read this, Jon Ossoff is getting absolutely pummeled by attack ads in Georgia. Republicans have spent more than _$10 million_ on TV and online already. It’s ridiculous.
Why so much money, this far from Election Day?
Because Jon Ossoff is the only Democratic Senator running for reelection in a state won by Trump. _That_ is why Republicans are hitting him so hard, so early in the campaign.
_And it’s why we’re coming to you with this request to help fight back._
If Jon is going to defend this seat, we _really_ need to keep hitting our fundraising goals, and donations to emails like this are a huge part of how it happens.
[**Can Jon Ossoff count on you to contribute $5, $50 or whatever you can afford to help him lay the foundation for a winning Senate reelection campaign here in Georgia?**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251219-1-f&t=1&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64)
**[DONATE NOW](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251219-1-f&t=2&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64)**
We wish we didn’t have to ask at all, but _Citizens United_ opened the dam for limitless dark money to flood into our elections.
_That’s a big part of why Congress is broken and corrupted. It’s why we have really expensive elections._
And also it’s why campaigns like ours have to build serious grassroots fundraising operations _from the very start._
Because if we don’t, we run the risk of our message getting drowned out by a flood of Super PAC spending.
So here’s the truth:
**Jon needs our help _today_ , Peter.**
_He is literally MAGA’s #1 target._
They will spare no expense and try every dirty trick in the book.
So yes, this election will be tough.
But this team knows how to win tough fights. We’ve done it before.
Let’s do it again.
[**Please, use this link to support Jon’s reelection campaign. Every donation counts. Every donation sends a powerful message to MAGA that we’re ready to fight and win.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251219-1-f&t=3&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[DONATE $5](https://act.electjon.com/go/34?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.PyZHDd&amount=5&refcode=em251219-1-f&t=4&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64)**
---
****
| **[DONATE OTHER](https://act.electjon.com/go/34?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.PyZHDd&refcode=em251219-1-f&t=5&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64)**
---
****
Thank you!
\- Team Ossoff

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=6&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=1299%2E5691347%2Ej0ZD64) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before.

No, this isn't about a campaign or an election.

I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society.

Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends.

[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=a0033567a81b49f38f79ca166a951e13&_e=RCHGEhAuLy7Lzn0aBNv-fb79sp8bhsk4CvWDF6sRPUUdcppzDGmVw6E3fBzlLMakzgIDnC8WLcOXgOYyVfePI4a5X_HmGJipnbURyYf5gBSOHwUyw1sJSpnU1hjnWHd7J5NjYUl5CMCoCRL8rbByG4nJG_ZWcsLQEL7cW9pxI6MhYb8RELF4Ufzjn1zHJojBk_8NZKlaSBTKbOU9yxGYhHz9h2XveBDVknnv0SZV-DAGK2n1zRHYhRxPRs_IXcf49baQsGGZxJP9RWwtbbkTILTqkUVPcR3YWwH325rpt4QiHPeqXCFqOO616z3vARgw1fpM7WWtDexPGrf1bczerAQLQTJNM4zd0pPM-JUZZ4d7CPOqt-7s-RMFxHamHXUlA5UlN2ntLTRqdbV0BVuKqX5bLZh5AXm_Y1z7g3p2JeVBHnJ454m4NtXrPQULff5sV-ZFEh0dTDma5HLT_2IzWwfxEkXs6m7RLQcFxSe1xr-0BzwiO4uRW7VE6A7gT6bzZsr0omRrUs7_MKMBofGvAK4WYk_6HrLbGh4M1CNc6FZTDWFeIvlJ93YIyXnxHMAz6R6isWcfKx6jCWSFeGfDpe-WNWSDSIGsNOms37-GT9byP-H0jBwXHUjYLk2gbybwwuv5Uv60moKyKRDVWUuyoRDw4r-iYZO090wULvi9FIvVT5xjBkHUiWQ0iaBXESRVUDOA0oFlBwYK0Ld94sECspghIdGnVTFdzhoJqJ3v3BqsJ_LG5TQa67Q8UCXsKgqD779J8oER095RMhe7Jt2jAO6eK4CgoL-e1JLhRrjBs5s%3D)

Please click here to donate $10, $50, $100, $500, $1,000, or ANY amount >>>](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=a0033567a81b49f38f79ca166a951e13&_e=RCHGEhAuLy7Lzn0aBNv-fb79sp8bhsk4CvWDF6sRPUUdcppzDGmVw6E3fBzlLMakzgIDnC8WLcOXgOYyVfePI4a5X_HmGJipnbURyYf5gBSOHwUyw1sJSpnU1hjnWHd7J5NjYUl5CMCoCRL8rbByG4nJG_ZWcsLQEL7cW9pxI6PX_KVO_kRErSG1Y8WhSAK1DZpfn_T6F4LukK9jnpVXBA3I8RY9zXIBHVuZlPH3mYcUs1hrZB99B6c6dCIy88FjTNtxRFcxObQBll8t1f3csQMFkP61g3DaR0c0SOeUnB0pAzpqIRp_0kt6LH_DGLmppKCi6MqlIr65e6s09rz5mTRPFuu3FkoH4dkkm3H8vf55-lkvj-iNqvCfzBcFbdt9jGughQODslzqrApz9P1bNQs8pPo5m_QfqUHSvcKzJSgOaEqRANBmjozIUreafGs3WCIQ39mIuZHJ-SSqsPS4nG7s0Ew1v8UiAbG9B2Mfqo3ZgeG3T5Xffw4XkA-VKoP3hJ-vQ_CrihciJQqBFHpZGklR7xfD1lSSpZOvSQZwxSdmhB5Ht_WQC0tkPOX-kkX6nBq5VK7bR4fWifghSA0ywAllKrGetNIR5cOyFhx35n1rLFK34EJAsvSUYs1TIgWwJrmhfhzps5qOspuPbhGGX1L9sNhtRb98O10ESVjF095Jr7sdxoy5gG0mkwtjlERf_tz9YasyeAar2hc476stE5l1AlrG2hE-a5UUutRkoDiJ8mPxXA6q57SCDK70oW2weZuKrMjE9biGq7KZ17d5YfT4ZxVgaQr07jFp-dAvk1Y%3D)**

Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important?

If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are _**desperate**_ to hear the unfiltered truth.

**This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for:**

* Free market capitalism
* Respect for achievement and success
* Free speech
* The future of young people and our great country

Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles.

Young people need an alternative.

The Atlas Society spreads Ayn Rand's message to millions of young people via social media, video content, graphic novels, conferences, and so much more.

I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 for The Atlas Society, which is why I am reaching out to you. Please consider making a donation by using one of the links I've copied below:

clean_body: This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before. No, this isn't about a campaign or an election. I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society. Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends. Today, she's the CEO of The Atlas Society, a non-profit organization whose mission is to spread Ayn Rand's message of capitalism, personal responsibility, reason, and entrepreneurship to young people. Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important? If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are desperate to hear the unfiltered truth. This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for: Free market capitalism Respect for achievement and success Free speech The future of young people and our great country Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles. Young people need an alternative. The Atlas Society spreads Ayn Rand's message to millions of young people via social media, video content, graphic novels, conferences, and so much more. I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 for The Atlas Society, which is why I am reaching out to you. Please consider making a donation by using one of the links I've copied below: You may be surprised, but when young people hear Ayn Rand's message of entrepreneurship, refusing to be a victim, and personal achievement, they are inspired and want to learn more. Yes, politics are important. But politics are downstream from culture. And culture is downstream from philosophy. The Atlas Society is on the frontlines of fighting in the philosophical culture war, which may determine the fate of the country's future. We CANNOT give up the culture or give up young people. This is more important than a campaign or a single election. You and I must invest in reaching young people with this message. Please don't wait to take action. My goal is to raise this $25,000 ASAP within a 48-hour period. Can I count on you to make a donation? Kellyanne Conway, Former Senior Counselor to President Trump The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

The Supreme Court is considering striking down a key plank of the Voting Rights Act. They could allow for the dismantling of congressional district maps that give people of color fair opportunities to elect the candidates of their choice. And that ruling could give Donald Trump and the GOP enough additional seats to lock in their party’s control of the House for a generation.
This all started when the Supreme Court took the extremely unusual step of rehearing a case that had covered narrower legal ground, giving them an opportunity to issue a more sweeping decision. The last time I can recall the Court rehearing an election-related case? Citizens United, which, of course, unleashed unlimited corporate money into our elections.
The timing of this case also reminds me of how the Court handled Citizens United. Usually, landmark rulings — like this one is poised to be — come in June. But the Court announced its Citizens United ruling in January 2010 — at the beginning of an election year. Now, it seems very likely that the Court’s ruling in this case could come by this January, giving Republican-run states enough time to gerrymander new maps ahead of the 2026 elections.
**Rigging control of the U.S. House is just one piece of Trump’s plans to attempt an autocratic takeover. **
It goes hand in hand with the voter suppression policies Republicans are enacting around the country. We could also see the Trump Regime deploying troops to more and more cities by the time voting starts — even using troops to close streets around certain polling places, resulting in longer lines and preventing communities from making their voices heard.
**Fair Fight is pushing back against this anti-democracy agenda by calling the nation’s attention to the damage the Supreme Court may inflict on our democracy, exposing and preventing voter suppression, making sure voters have the information they need to get to the ballot box, helping elect voting rights champions, and organizing against the rise of authoritarianism. **
**[So I’m asking if you can help ensure the strength and survival of our democracy by donating to Fair Fight today. Your support makes it possible to stay vigilant against new threats, and that’s only becoming more and more critical as MAGA escalates their attempts to undermine free and fair elections.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121582318/589925195/-291334846?refcode=emm_ff_20251219_T1_MarcEliasNOR_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZjU1ZDZiZi1lM2RjLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5Suc1VmrPs4lVRTReZ9txfkuoreGWue4fHbQKkcO-i4=) **
Thank you for everything you do as a member of this movement. It all adds up, and when we stick together for these fights, we win.
Marc Elias

name: Tim Kaine
subject: What Virginia showed us, and what comes next
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Friends,
We’re down to the final two weeks of the year, and this is a moment to take stock of what we’ve accomplished and to decide how we want to head into the fight ahead.
Here in Virginia, voters sent a powerful message this November. We rejected Donald Trump’s extremism and chaos, and we elected leaders who will stand up for working families, protect our rights, and move our Commonwealth forward.
Those victories weren’t just important for Virginia — they were a clear preview of what’s possible nationwide in 2026 . A rebuke of Trump’s disastrous administration, and a reminder that when we organize, invest early, and show up with energy, we can win.
In less than a year, we have the chance to break the GOP’s grip on Washington, end their trifecta of power, and build a stronger check on Trump’s extremism. But that won’t happen without hard work, and it won’t happen without building our momentum right now.
What we do in these final days of 2025 will determine how strong we are in the new year — whether we’re playing catch-up, or building toward a real blue wave.
We can elect and re-elect leaders who will fight for our priorities and keep this country from going further down a dangerous path.
If you’re with me, please chip in any amount today to my Common Ground PAC so we have the resources we need to take back the Senate in 2026. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?amount=25]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=100]
OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
If we stand strong and work together, I believe we can win big in 2026. Thank you for staying engaged, believing in this work, and helping close out the year strong.
— Tim Kaine

name: The Grant Institute
subject: Grant Report: December 19, 2025
body:
Election News Update from the Grant Institute

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/6a1865cd0df8/grant-report-september-12-12770802?e=c16f400a56)
---
|
---
| | | | | | **The Long Shadow of** _**Bush v. Gore**_ **By Justin Riemer, President, USGI, and Marshal Trigg, Junior Counsel, USGI**
Twenty-five years ago this month, the Supreme Court decided _Bush v. Gore_ , settling a presidential election determined by 537 votes out of nearly six million cast in Florida—a margin of 0.009%.
The 2000 election was infamous for, among other things, “hanging chads,” but the broader lesson was about how the most technical flaws in election processes can suddenly become decisive in a close race. Palm Beach County’s butterfly ballot design confused many voters, and Duval County’s split-page ballot caused thousands of overvotes. These issues affected only a small percentage of votes, but in such a close election, everything mattered.
The outcome of that election was highly divisive. Yet a resounding bipartisan consensus quickly emerged that we needed to fix persistent systemic weaknesses in election administration to avoid a repeat of 2000.
Congress responded with the Help America Vote Act (HAVA) of 2002. HAVA upgraded and modernized voting systems by replacing punch-card machines and ensured systems allowed voters to verify their selections and detect overvotes. It also required states to offer provisional ballots. Since 2003, states have received approximately [$4.35 billion in HAVA funds](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=400b5d4d45&e=c16f400a56).
Recognizing that accurate voter rolls are a cornerstone to effective election administration, HAVA also mandated statewide computerized registration lists. The National Voter Registration Act (NVRA) of 1993 had already established some national registration standards, requiring states to offer registration at DMVs and public assistance agencies and take certain steps to maintain clean voter rolls.
It is hard to argue that the NVRA reflected the same truly bipartisan process as HAVA. President George H.W. Bush vetoed an earlier version of the bill and only a few Republicans [voted](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=a1ecdcf3fe&e=c16f400a56) for its final passage. Although the NVRA explicitly [purported](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=44d7530e97&e=c16f400a56)[ ](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=73717ec9dd&e=c16f400a56)to balance access and integrity, few conservatives would agree. It is not a wonder that the NVRA has left, for many, a 30-plus year bad aftertaste.
Perhaps it shouldn’t be surprising then that a Republican Congress is looking at changes. The Committee on House Administration has held a series of hearings looking at how to modernize and improve the NVRA, especially its list maintenance provisions. Last week, its Subcommittee on Elections [examined](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4f29071616&e=c16f400a56) the issue following the full committee’s [July hearing](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=0cf20c5dec&e=c16f400a56) where USGI President Justin Riemer testified on the NVRA’s weaknesses.
A few common threads emerged. One is the 90-day pre-election period prohibiting systematic voter removals, cited by both Florida State Law Professor Michael Morley and Riemer. Morley noted the NVRA’s ambiguity regarding what activities can take place during the 90-day blackout period. Does “remove” refer to the process of inactivating a voter or removing them from the voter rolls completely? He also urged Congress to clarify that the prohibition on “systematic” removals does not reach individualized determinations on ineligibility. Both witnesses stressed the blackout applies to primary _and_ general elections, thus limiting systematic removals for half, or more, of each federal election year.
Witnesses also highlighted how courts have inconsistently interpreted other ambiguous NVRA provisions, including that list maintenance be “uniform” and “nondiscriminatory” and that states make a “reasonable effort” to remove ineligible voters.
The hearings also reflect how the NVRA is showing its age. It fails to specifically address removing or screening out noncitizen registrants (much less of a concern in 1993), electronic transactions, and heavily relies on using USPS mail processes for list maintenance.
Expectedly, the Democratic minority witness last week emphasized how the NVRA helped increase voter registration rates and warned about aggressive list maintenance practices. And there was at least some overlap across the aisle on modernization: the Democratic witness, along with Republican witness Mark Braden, floated amending the NVRA to require states to offer online voter registration.
Given the tight majorities in Congress and the intense ideological divide on voting issues, NVRA reforms may require unrealistic compromises. Democratic proposals for automatic or election day registration are likely nonstarters, at least without accompanying robust eligibility verification requirements. And progressives will undoubtedly bristle at Republican demands to enhance the NVRA’s list maintenance requirements. Still, the fact that Republicans are entertaining federal reforms is notable. For years, Republican conventional wisdom has maintained that reopening the NVRA framework carries more risk than upside. But persistent frustrations with the NVRA appear to be pushing some to reconsider.
Beyond the partisan angling, states are increasingly pressed to answer the same practical questions: how should we verify eligibility and keep rolls accurate in an era of greater population mobility? And how can we better leverage technology, particularly data-sharing capabilities between government agencies? The world has changed. It took a rude wake up call for Congress to recognize this 25 years ago—maybe it will consider acting before another one.
---
|
---
| | **Alabama**
[Secretary of State Allen](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d6023de1d6&e=c16f400a56) supports legislation that would tighten disclosures for voter registration information and cap fees on voter list data. The proposal seeks to limit the sharing of sensitive personally identifiable information for commercial use as well.
**Alaska**
The [Division of Elections](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=513acc550a&e=c16f400a56) mailed “voter registration confirmation notices” as part of its annual list maintenance process to verify whether registered voters still reside at their listed addresses. Officials say this outreach, required by state law and the National Voter Registration Act, helps ensure the voter rolls remain accurate ahead of future elections.
**District of Columbia**
The D.C. Council voted down a proposal to delay implementation of [ranked-choice voting](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=01b0579227&e=c16f400a56), clearing the way for the city’s first use of the system in the June 2026 mayoral and council primaries. The effort to postpone the rollout failed 8-5.
**Georgia**
The Secretary of State’s office said it is [investigating](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=16d875548b&e=c16f400a56)[ ](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=12bc048116&e=c16f400a56)a poll worker accused of scanning multiple ballots cast by a family member during a recent runoff election. Election officials reported the worker did not follow proper procedures by handling a relative’s ballot more than once, prompting review of the circumstances and possible charges.
**Illinois**
The [Will County Clerk](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=134737f2d6&e=c16f400a56) said the office will not accept mobile or digital driver’s licenses as valid ID for voter services requiring ID. Instead, individuals must present a physical form of ID that meets state requirements. County officials said this interpretation reflects their assessment of current law and guidance.
**Indiana**
The state [Senate](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=b1b8e40d89&e=c16f400a56) voted down a proposed redistricting plan that would have redrawn legislative districts ahead of the 2026 elections. The defeat came after Republican leaders failed to secure enough support from within their own caucus. Opponents of the proposal argued the map favored incumbents and lacked sufficient bipartisan input, forcing lawmakers back to the drawing board.
**Kansas**
[Joe Ceballos](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d0fa2bbe80&e=c16f400a56) resigned as mayor of Coldwater after being charged with illegally voting in multiple elections and related offenses due to his noncitizen status. Prosecutors say the case followed an investigation into his voting history, and charges include voting without being qualified and election perjury. The situation drew nationwide attention as a rare criminal allegation tied to a local elected official’s conduct.
**Maryland**
Republicans from the Committee on House Administration sent a letter demanding answers from the State Board of Elections after voter registration records showed Ian Andre Roberts, an illegal alien and former Iowa school superintendent [arrested by ICE](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=173d207fb1&e=c16f400a56)[,](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e3b92e26b7&e=c16f400a56) was listed as a registered voter in Prince George’s County. Previously, the county had released the unredacted voter records for Roberts, following a legal threat by Restoring Integrity and Elections (RITE) and the American Accountability Foundation (AAF).
**Minnesota**
So far 113,047 Minnesotans have opted into a [2023 law](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=2578153161&e=c16f400a56) that lets voters join a permanent absentee ballot list, meaning they automatically receive a mail ballot for every election without reapplying.
**Mississippi**
A [Leflore County](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=866f3b26c8&e=c16f400a56) woman was convicted under a ballot harvesting law for collecting and transmitting an absentee ballot for another person and received a six-month suspended sentence plus a $2,718 fine. She says she did nothing wrong helping senior citizens with their ballots and has filed an appeal arguing with how the statute was applied. The case stems from actions during the 2023 Democratic primaries under a law that bars non-family members from submitting ballots on others’ behalf.
**Ohio**
The state joined a [new election data sharing network](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=06c715b265&e=c16f400a56) called EleXa with nine other states to compare voter registration information to help identify voters with registrations in multiple states and improve voter-roll accuracy. Secretary of State LaRose said the agreements are intended to catch illegal out-of-state voting and maintain accurate rolls after Ohio left the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC) in 2023.
The Secretary of State’s office launched the [Election Readiness Training Institute](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5446734f68&e=c16f400a56) to provide ongoing training and credentialing for election officials across the state. The program requires newly appointed election board members to complete specific training credits within their first year and requires continuing education annually for incumbents. It centralizes instructional content and resources to help ensure officials stay current on laws and administration practices.
**Pennsylvania**
[County election directors](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=6530a3c745&e=c16f400a56) say problems with third-party voter registration drives, including large batches of incomplete or falsified forms, create administrative burdens and can overwhelm local offices ahead of deadlines. Following the Attorney General charging six individuals with falsifying registration forms, officials want changes to how registration organizations operate to reduce invalid submissions and ease processing work for elections staff.
**Tennessee**
The Dickson County Election Commission [appointed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=514353dba6&e=c16f400a56) a new elections administrator at a recent meeting, but two of the five commissioners raised concerns about how the selection process was conducted.
**Texas**
Following the voter list technology upgrade at the Secretary of State’s office, their candidate portal was part of another technology update, also [referred to as TEAM](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=8b6c560517&e=c16f400a56). This created issues with counties trying to find candidates who were qualifying for the upcoming primary.
**Virginia**
A state lawmaker is [proposing legislation](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=02fbb2cfe3&e=c16f400a56) to put Virginia back into ERIC following the state’s withdrawal in May 2023.
**Wisconsin**
The [Elections Commission](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=e1f5ad6085&e=c16f400a56) voted 5 to 1 to decline a Department of Justice demand for the state’s unredacted voter registration file, saying state law prohibits sharing sensitive information such as partial Social Security numbers, driver’s license numbers, and dates of birth. The letter to DOJ noted the commission can only provide publicly available, redacted voter information, and said the request exceeds what state law allows.
A [new law](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=14f51617ae&e=c16f400a56) allows small towns to hire election clerks who live outside municipal limits without waiting for the next election cycle, a change clerks say will help address persistent staffing shortages. Gov. Tony Evers also vetoed a separate bill that would have broadened who can appeal election-related decisions to court.
**Wyoming**
The Legislature’s Joint Corporations, Elections & Political Subdivisions Committee [voted 11-2](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=f62a5c63bf&e=c16f400a56) to sponsor a bill that would legally specify who is permitted to return another voter’s absentee ballot, potentially making this the 41st state with such a law. The measure is expected to be considered during the 2026 legislative session.
---
|
---
| | **DOJ Voter Data Lawsuits**
DOJ [expanded its voter data enforcement campaign again](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=81797ca4e9&e=c16f400a56) on December 12, filing new lawsuits against Colorado, Hawaii, Massachusetts, and Nevada for allegedly failing to produce their statewide voter registration lists upon request. And just [yesterday](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=22f47fd6d3&e=c16f400a56), it also sued D.C., Georgia, Illinois, and Wisconsin. These new filings bring the nationwide total to 21 states and D.C. sued over voter roll disclosure. DOJ also filed a separate lawsuit against Fulton County, Georgia, seeking records related to the 2020 election.
**Arizona**
_**RNC v. Fontes**_**, No. cv-2024-050553 (Ariz. Super. Ct. Maricopa Cnty.)**
On December 5, the Arizona Court of Appeals [dismissed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=fba63c9a9d&e=c16f400a56) a Republican lawsuit challenging key provisions of the state’s Elections Procedures Manual (EPM). The panel ruled that the plaintiffs lacked legal standing to pursue their case which alleged that the EPM was unlawfully promulgated and contradicted several Arizona election statutes. The case has been partially sponsored by RITE PAC.
**California**
_**Tangipa v. Newsom**_ , **No. 2:25-cv-10616 (C.D. Cal.)**
A three-judge federal panel in the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California heard arguments this week in a challenge seeking to block California’s new congressional district maps adopted via Proposition 50. The GOP challengers asked the court for a preliminary injunction.
**Delaware**
_**ACLU of Del. v. Town of Fenwick Island**_**, No. N25C-12-033 CAK (Del. Super. Ct.)**
On December 4, the ACLU of Delaware [filed suit](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=8e03a46d58&e=c16f400a56) in Delaware Superior Court challenging the Town of Fenwick Island’s practice of allowing artificial entities such as corporations to register and cast votes in municipal elections.
**Maine**
_**United States v. Bellows**_**, No. 1:25-cv-1379 (D. Me.)**
On December 12, Maine [filed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=8a460a18a8&e=c16f400a56) a motion to dismiss the federal lawsuit brought by the U.S. Department of Justice seeking access to its detailed voter registration data.
**Maryland**
_**RNC v. DeMarinis**_**, No. 1:25-cv-3989 (D. Md.)**
On December 5, the RNC [filed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=f75aafea4d&e=c16f400a56) a federal lawsuit against Maryland election alleging violations of the National Voter Registration Act (NVRA) by failing to maintain adequate list maintenance procedures. The lawsuit states that at least two of Maryland’s largest counties report voter-registration totals exceeding the number of adult citizens aged 18 and over.
**Michigan**
_**Allen v. City of Hamtramck**_**, No. 25-___-CK (Wayne Cnty. Cr. Ct.)**
A Hamtramck city clerk [filed a lawsuit](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=68cb88b36b&e=c16f400a56) in state court against the City of Hamtramck and various city officials, alleging retaliation and related claims after she was placed on administrative leave in the aftermath of the contested November 4 mayoral election. The plaintiff contends she was suspended in retaliation for reporting alleged election misconduct and fraud to the Michigan Attorney General’s office earlier in the year.
**Missouri**
_**People Not Politicians v. Hoskins**_**, No. 25AC-CC07128 (Mo. Cir. Ct. Cole Cnty.)**
On December 12, a state court [paused litigation](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=16b1f9efb5&e=c16f400a56) over whether Missouri Secretary of State Hoskins can decline to count thousands of signatures collected on a referendum petition aimed at overturning the state’s newly enacted congressional redistricting map. The suit challenges Hoskins’ position that signatures gathered before October 14 should not count toward qualifying the referendum for the November 2026 ballot.
**Montana**
_**Chippewa Cree Indians of the Rocky Boy’s Rsrv. v. Chouteau Cnty.**_**, No. 4:25-cv-00069-BMM (D. Mont.)**
On December 15, 2025, a federal court [approved a settlement](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=64df3a6680&e=c16f400a56) that requires Chouteau County to redraw its commission districts to create a single-member district encompassing the Rocky Boy’s Reservation. Plaintiffs had argued that the at-large voting system diluted the voting strength of tribal voters.
**Nevada**
_**United States v. Aguilar**_**, No. 3:25-cv-00728 (D. Nev.)**
On December 16, the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) and its Nevada chapter [filed a motion to intervene](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=8faf2ec641&e=c16f400a56) in the DOJ’s federal lawsuit against Nevada election officials over access to the state’s detailed voter registration data.
**New Hampshire**
_**Robertson v. Scanlan**_**, No. 218-2025-CV-000951 (N.H. Super. Ct. Rockingham Cnty.)**
On December 12, a state court [dismissed](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1ee35d7912&e=c16f400a56) a challenge brought by three voters against New Hampshire’s new absentee ballot identification requirements. The plaintiffs argued the statute imposes undue burdens and discriminates against voters with disabilities. The court ruled that the law imposes reasonable, nondiscriminatory obligations and does not unconstitutionally burden the right to vote.
**New Jersey**
_**Barberio v. Desai**_**, No. L.-002675-25 (N.J. Super. Ct. Morris Cnty.)**
On December 5, the incumbent mayor of Parsippany [filed a legal challenge](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=c61d185a68&e=c16f400a56) in Morris County Superior Court contesting the results of the town’s November 4, 2025 mayoral election after Democrat Pulkit Desai was certified the winner by just 80 votes. Barberio’s complaint alleges discrepancies and irregularities in the vote-by-mail count, including a purported mismatch between the number of mail-in ballots tallied and those officially recorded as received by the deadline, as well as counting errors by the Morris County Board of Canvassers.
_**In re Tom Malinowski**_**, Nos. A-3542-21, A-3543-21 (N.J. Super. Ct. App. Div.)**
The state Supreme Court [declined to hear a challenge](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=1a9625d7e4&e=c16f400a56) to the state’s longstanding ban on fusion voting, leaving intact lower-court rulings upholding the prohibition. The case sought to invalidate New Jersey election laws that prevent multiple political parties from nominating the same candidate and having that candidate appear on the ballot under more than one party line.
**Ohio**
_**NRSC v. FEC**_**, No. 1:22-cv-00639 (S.D. Ohio)**
On December 9, the Supreme Court [heard oral argument](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3aca8af41f&e=c16f400a56) in a major constitutional challenge to federal campaign-finance limits on coordinated party expenditures. A decision is expected by late June or early July.
**Pennsylvania**
_**United States v. Laiss**_**, No. 25-386 (E.D. Pa.)**
On December 8, a federal court [heard argument](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d7b2517136&e=c16f400a56) on whether a broad presidential pardon issued by President Donald Trump should be construed to cover the federal double-voting and voter-fraud charges against defendant Matthew Laiss. Laiss is accused of voting twice in the 2020 general election — once by mail in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, and once in person in Florida — allegedly casting both votes for Trump.
**Rhode Island**
_**United States v. Amore**_**, No. 1:25-cv-00639 (D.R.I.)**
On December 9, Common Cause Rhode Island [moved to intervene](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=da1d9c0d32&e=c16f400a56) as a defendant in the DOJ’s lawsuit seeking access to Rhode Island’s statewide voter-registration database and related election records.
**South Carolina**
_**NAACP S.C. State Conference v. Wilson**_**, No. 3:25-cv-13754 (D.S.C.)**
On December 5, the NAACP and three voters [filed a federal lawsuit](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d02d82079f&e=c16f400a56) challenging state voting assistance restrictions that plaintiffs say unlawfully burden voters with disabilities. The plaintiffs allege that existing state law severely limits who may assist voters with disabilities in requesting or returning absentee ballots and imposes felony penalties on those who provide help outside narrow categories.
**Wisconsin**
_**United States v. Town of Thornapple**_**, No. 3:24-CV-00664 (W.D. Wis.)**
On December 12, the town of Thornapple [reached a settlement](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=d868a358cb&e=c16f400a56) with DOJ to provide at least one voting system accessible to voters with disabilities at each polling place, resolving DOJ lawsuit that followed the town’s 2024 decision to prohibit electronic voting machines in federal elections.
---
| [Click here for more on RITE’s active litigation](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=4e33f99370&e=c16f400a56)
---
|
---
| | The U.S. House Committee on Administration’s Elections Subcommittee held a hearing on December 10 “Examining Potential Updates to the NVRA.” A video of the proceedings and witness statements are available [here](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=20379021ce&e=c16f400a56).
The American Enterprise Institute recently sponsored a daylong conference marking the 25th Anniversary of the _Bush v. Gore_ decision. A replay of the conference is available [here](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=3783d7d978&e=c16f400a56). AEI’s John Fortier and EAC Commissioner Don Palmer also hosted Michael Carvin, a key player in the litigation, on their [Voting Booth podcast](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=9756842c5c&e=c16f400a56).
An [NPR story](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=6fcce9fcc9&e=c16f400a56) states that the expanded federal Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) database, now used by some jurisdictions to check citizenship, has incorrectly flagged U.S. citizens as non-citizens and possibly led to wrongful removal from voter lists and added documentation requests. Critics argue SAVE was not designed for voter eligibility verification and that errors could disenfranchise eligible voters if states rely on its results without additional safeguards.
---
|
---
| | [Richard Pildes](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=000858729d&e=c16f400a56) looks at how the U.S. Supreme Court allowed Texas’s mid-decade congressional map to stand for the 2026 midterms and how the ruling could discourage federal courts from blocking states’ late changes to election laws or maps. The opinion emphasized that lower federal courts should not intervene too close to an election, potentially giving state legislatures greater latitude to enact or revise election rules well before ballots are cast. Observers worry this interpretation of the “Purcell” principle could make it harder to challenge controversial election changes in federal court ahead of 2026.
---
|
---
| | [Thessalia Merivaki and Ioannis Ziogas](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=914cafa32f&e=c16f400a56)'s paper provides an overview of how artificial intelligence is reshaping election administration, voter information, and campaign environments. It identifies key risks, including AI-generated misinformation, synthetic candidates or voter contacts, and vulnerabilities in AI-assisted administrative tools, and evaluates their implications for election officials. The authors outline policy options for states and federal agencies to strengthen preparedness, improve transparency, and mitigate emerging AI-related threats to election integrity.
The [Bipartisan Policy Center](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=0c0e27ff8f&e=c16f400a56) paper explains a DHS proposal to incorporate state motor-vehicle records into the federal Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE) system, which election officials increasingly use to check voter citizenship. It outlines how SAVE works, discusses privacy and accuracy risks tied to driver’s-license data, and stresses that new data sources might increase inconclusive or incorrect matches that election offices must resolve.
Computer Science Professor [Andrew Appel](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=843cd5fa15&e=c16f400a56) finds that “VoteSecure,” a proposal to enable voting by smartphone, suffers from fundamental vulnerabilities that make secure and verifiable mobile voting unattainable with current technology. The author explains that _end-to-end_ verification methods still risk exposing voter preferences, threatening ballot secrecy or failing to prevent malware and server manipulation. The piece underscores that all known internet-voting protocols, including the one advocated by the Mobile Voting Foundation, have gaps and impracticalities that render them too insecure for public elections.

---

Disney is hiring a [Senior Counsel for Political Advertising](https://grantdemocracy.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e037df946273cbd1eaa641b78&id=5a7332afa3&e=c16f400a56).

**Finally, please note that the Grant Report will be taking a holiday hiatus and resume on January 9, 2026. We wish all our readers a restful and joyous holiday season.**
