name: Hoosiers for Haggard
subject: Just Wanted to Wish You a Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays!
body:
From my family to yours, enjoy this holiday season!

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/65fde2c09266/just-wanted-to-wish-you-a-merry-christmas-and-happy-holidays?e=72dace1453)
---
|
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| |
# Merry Christmas and Happy Holidays!
---
|
---
| | Last week I had the honor of attending the Indiana Society of Washington Holiday Reception in Washington DC. It was amazing to meet with some of our great Republican leaders and talk what we need to promote our great conservative agenda into 2026. I can not wait until I will work for the people when elected next year!

---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | It can not be an email without mentioning a parade so in this case I wanted to shoutout the amazing people of Monticello for putting an their annual Christmas parade through their downtown. Although it was colder than we hoped for, we still showed off the new lighted up jet and handed out plenty of candy!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | From my family to yours, Merry Christmas and happy holidays these next few weeks. Although the weather seems to looking like warm and dry, we hope you stay safe and enjoy your new year! Can’t wait to talk to you in 2026!
---
|
---
|
---
| | I will continue highlighting the endorsements I continue to receive throughout the district from local leaders and groups that put their faith in me to represent them in Congress. Our momentum is just getting started and I can’t wait to continue to share with you who is already on board. If you ever want to get involved or endorse, please reach out!
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
|
---
| [Check out our website here!](https://hoosiersforhaggard.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b9e7cbafb97c2110b065cece3&id=7e3264e0dc&e=72dace1453)

body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1446%2E251025%2Eu44y7W ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1446%2E251025%2Eu44y7W ]Abdul for US Senate

Breaking news,
Peter: Our first TV ad is
launching TODAY with a six-figure buy.

[ A ]A screenshot of the new TV ad, featuring Abdul and Bernie Sanders

Before we say anything else, we want to be honest with you — running ads
like this is expensive. The only way we can afford this is through
receiving grassroots contributions from people like you.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/507?c0=em-ms-aes-20251219-1-ak.8Ns67e&amount=10&refcode=em20251219-1444&t=1004&akid=1446%2E251025%2Eu44y7W ]Can you chip in right now to help us keep running ads like this one?
This strategy only works if our ads stay up long enough for voters to
actually see them.

name: Joe Jaworski
subject: What it will take
disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
I’m going to be direct, because this moment demands it. If we are serious about stopping the people who are running Texas into the ground... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14191584?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=f8c36f1615&e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | , I’m going to be blunt because the moment demands it. If we are going to stop the people who are running Texas into the ground, **we have to come together****now**. There is no cavalry coming. There is no magic moment later where this gets easier. If we want to beat MAGA extremism, corruption, and cruelty in Texas, we have to build the power to do it ourselves. **This campaign is doing the work.** We’re organizing statewide, showing up in communities, earning endorsements, and making the case for honest, competent leadership. But none of that can continue at the scale required without the resources to support it. Winning a competitive Democratic primary for Texas Attorney General takes planning, travel, staff, communication, and early voter outreach. Those things are not optional and they are not free. **I’m ready for this fight, and I’m unafraid of it. What I need now is for people who believe Texas deserves better to stand together and invest in this effort.** [**If you are with me, I need you now. Please make a contribution today so we have the strength to win and the ability to stop what’s happening to our state.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=56c0da4ad9&e=5d8d80652b) Onward!

**
**

name: Elizabeth
subject: Let’s keep the momentum going!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
| | | Friend — Remember that feeling the night Democrats swept Virginia in November? _**I do, too.**_ We built incredible momentum in 2025 — but momentum only matters if we keep it alive. Trump’s allies understand exactly how high the stakes are. Republicans are targeting my frontline swing seat because they know that if they can flip districts like mine, they can rewrite the rules, redraw the maps, and roll back decades of progress. **But we’re not going to let them.** _Not on voting rights.__
Not on reproductive freedom.
Not on LGBTQ+ equality.
__Not on living wages or public education or any of the values Virginia families depend on._ We are the firewall. But a firewall only works if it’s strong enough. I need your help right now to build the resources to withstand the attacks coming our way. [**Can you rush a donation today to keep this seat blue and keep Virginia standing strong against Trump and MAGA extremism?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqeoZfkhx0rJYxvzeQyPAOXxPzaHpN7uxnA66Ao0W-7EqZyFw6iLW1nc6uNsMh9U_6IQObms1r6C9PcNWYamJT1Ny329FiYYAQ_RCBu8QIG-LSffKioFS6X44PaxFhuaiFYN_w0ZeM4VnjY5TdhnjjpUAGctYnicmA6EIT1XwnRscIiaTr8Z_IGWSOqwvbVllkkq2mcGZemGf3Y2OLx51P1JK2OWFm5YiJGLemmZUNmQHqUHTkdwRchELGHyyDAuFI_iuVxT5oCY5VUBYQuCiixoUfJz1ONp7oBfIW9_ev3U_/4mk/3vmuTyFDTBKLH2Cg-v7pHQ/h0/h001.fB9FwVInANHGac31aJJbMdrYP1XPiIMaNgLhkRb5ohU)****_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately._ [Donate $10

clean_body: | | | Friend — Remember that feeling the night Democrats swept Virginia in November? I do, too. We built incredible momentum in 2025 — but momentum only matters if we keep it alive. Trump’s allies understand exactly how high the stakes are. Republicans are targeting my frontline swing seat because they know that if they can flip districts like mine, they can rewrite the rules, redraw the maps, and roll back decades of progress. But we’re not going to let them. Not on voting rights. Not on reproductive freedom. Not on LGBTQ+ equality. Not on living wages or public education or any of the values Virginia families depend on. We are the firewall. But a firewall only works if it’s strong enough. I need your help right now to build the resources to withstand the attacks coming our way. you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. This is the moment we draw the line. This is where we show Trump’s movement that Virginia will not back down. Not today. Not in 2026. Not ever. ** Thanks for your support. — Elizabeth | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Friends of Elizabeth Guzman PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman. Contributions or gifts to Guzman for Congress are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Elizabeth Guzman, please .

clean_body: We’d like to submit your response to Trump before midnight! | | | | What’s your current level of satisfaction with President Donald Trump? A. 100% B. 75% C. 50% D. 25% Hi, It’s Team Trump. Now that 2025 is coming to a close we’re requesting that EVERYONE WHO VOTED FOR TRUMP in 2016, 2020, and/or 2024 kindly respond before the end of the business day! We’d personally give President Trump an A + but we need to hear from you. If you respond ASAP, we’ll even give you the opportunity to leave a note for President Trump that we’ll hand-deliver to him! Thank you for your swift response! Together, we will make America greater than anyone's ever seen. Sincerely, Team Trump | | | | | | | | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. All contributions are subject to the limits and prohibitions of the Federal Election Campaign Act. Contributions from corporations, foreign nationals (i.e., without “green cards”), federal government contractors, and other federally impermissible sources are strictly prohibited. Contributions made in the name of, or refunded by, any other person are unlawful. | Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc., P.O. Box 509, Arlington, VA 22216. Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate’s Committee.

name: VoteVets HQ
subject: $1776 of bullsh*t
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
That money was allocated by Congress. It was part of a $2.9 billion allocation to supplement the basic allowance for housing — money provided to help them afford their rent. Trump had to give that money out, and he’s just putting his name on the check.
#
One of the only intelligible things Trump said in his flop of an address last night was that he was sending $1776 checks to everyone on active duty in the Military.
Warrior Dividends, he called it. What a HERO. So brave.
One problem — it’s complete bullsh•t.
That money was allocated by Congress. It was part of a $2.9 billion allocation to supplement the basic allowance for housing — money provided to help them afford their rent. Trump had to give that money out, and he’s just putting his name on the check.
That ain’t all. News just broke that the VA is eliminating 35,000 health care jobs. They left thousands and thousands of positions for doctors and nurses unfilled this year, and instead of doing the hiring and getting care to Veterans, they’re just eliminating the jobs altogether. Problem solved!
That is, unless you’re one of the vets waiting on hold, sitting in the waiting room for hours, or unable to even schedule an appointment. They’re working around the clock to strangle the VA so they can sell it off to the highest bidder and make millions off of privatization. Reliable, quality care and services for Veterans can’t stand in the way of shareholder value.
One thing you can count on: There’s always a grift. Always.
But don’t worry. There’s a reckoning coming, and it hits next November. We’re more fired up than ever to hand eviction notices to every MAGA member of Congress after we flip the House and Senate back to blue next year.
VoteVets won’t quit until it happens.
[**Please, we’re humbly asking you to make a donation and support that work.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-dec25.3jrL2s&refcode=em251219-1-160&t=1&akid=34827%2E3013755%2E7FCOem)

name: The Antimonopolist
subject: Could the US win the AI race, but lose the war for economic preeminence?
body:
(Here is a piece I wrote for the Financial Times about the geopolitics of artificial intelligence. If you are still looking for a Christmas or Holiday gift, consider my new book "The Age of Extraction" recently on New Yorker Magazine's list of best books of 2025). Over the past year, major US tech companies have spent more than $350bn on AI-related infrastructure, with projections of over $400bn for 2026. This far exceeds the spending of any other nation -- most notably China, where total invest

[The Antimonopolist](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/bac0e8b2?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
| [Could the US win the AI race, but lose the war for economic preeminence?](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/bfba0e8d?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---
| By Tim Wu • 19 Dec 2025 | [View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/0c3ffc3c?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
---|---
[View in browser](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/52f20cdf?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21)
| | | (Here is a piece I wrote for the [Financial Times](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/5dd2a08a?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) about the geopolitics of artificial intelligence. If you are still looking for a Christmas or Holiday gift, consider my new book ["The Age of Extraction" ](https://the-antimonopolist.ghost.io/r/1b9a05b0?m=46b412a0-1cbe-4fcb-b85d-2477ce9f4e21) recently on New Yorker Magazine's list of best books of 2025).

Over the past year, major US tech companies have spent more than $350bn on AI-related infrastructure, with projections of over $400bn for 2026. This far exceeds the spending of any other nation -- most notably China, where total investment is closer to an estimated $100bn. For many in the West, it may be reassuring that we have companies bold enough and capital markets deep enough to dominate a spending contest. If artificial intelligence is -- as prophesied -- the one ring to rule them all, then it would seem the west has the future in hand. That is the optimistic story. Yet there is another possibility: that Silicon Valley's obsession with AI could mean winning the AI race but losing a broader contest for economic pre-eminence. That follows because the US has gone all-in on AI, while China is spreading its bets across several plausible futures. It all depends on the bet on AI being the right one. Despite all the talk of an existential AI race, China is somewhat less committed to AI than sometimes portrayed. Beijing regularly describes AI as a "national strategic priority" and has invested to avoid falling too far behind. But the state and its major companies are spending much more money to secure dominance in other domains, such as electric vehicles, batteries, robotics, solar panels, wind turbines and other forms of advanced manufacturing. These sectors may be less glamorous, but their returns are far less speculative.

It is the US that is truly infatuated with AI, with investments influenced by goals that are as mystical as they are commercial, especially the pursuit of Artificial General Intelligence and "the singularity". There is a strong belief in continued exponential progress -- a rarity in the history of technology. The deeper one digs, the more otherworldly it becomes, among both AI proponents and doomsayers. The concentrated, monopolised nature of the US tech sector adds to the risk: with so much spending power in so few hands, the danger of groupthink grows.

What to do? If the pay-off from AI is uncertain, the prudent strategy would involve diversification and hedging. But American venture capital is as fixated on AI as the tech platforms themselves, and financial markets have rewarded the current course. The public sector could hedge, but the US -- under Trump -- has cut back support for clean-energy investment, leaving the national tech strategy looking like a large wager on a single horse. Today's AI spending, while impressive, is undeniably a frontloaded bet on one vision of the future. It may prove inspired and visionary. Or it could be remembered as an overdone fixation on a technology whose utility is narrower than advertised. Its failure to deliver could also be the trigger for a destabilising stock crash that leaves the west behind. Seen from some distance, the American tech strategy looks like a straightforward syllogism: AI is the most important technology of the 21st century; it is extremely expensive to build; therefore, whoever spends the most will be the dominant civilisation of the future. But any syllogism stands or falls with its premises. The key question is whether today's version of AI is in fact the most important path to prosperity and a better future. That turns out to be more of a leap of faith than many assume. The business case has long been somewhat vague, and on closer examination much of what Silicon Valley presents as the goals of AI has a distinctly fantastic flavour. "We believe Artificial Intelligence is our alchemy, our Philosopher's Stone," writes venture capitalist Marc Andreessen. He and Silicon Valley's other "accelerationists" believe achieving artificial intelligence is the paramount goal for humanity, a kind of secular rapture. Believers think AI can solve most of our problems: just last week, Google DeepMind's Demis Hassabis suggested AI could invent free renewable clean energy and discover the cure for most diseases; humanity is then "travelling to the stars and spreading consciousness to the galaxy".

In some ways, the American AI race looks not unlike the race to build the grandest cathedrals in late medieval Europe. Then, the motives were a mixture of pride, economics and a sincere religious belief the structures would bring humanity closer to salvation. The byproduct was some extraordinary architecture -- but also financial ruin for cities such as Beauvais and Cologne, overwhelmed by their own ambition. Inside Silicon Valley, the finish line of the "race" is the achievement of AGI: the moment when machines acquire humanlike cognitive versatility. Believers argue that once AGI arrives, it will trigger further breakthroughs -- the "law of accelerating returns". A superintelligence may then reach "the singularity", a concept popularised by Ray Kurzweil and Vernor Vinge, ushering in a regime described by the latter as "as radically different from our human past as we humans are from the lower animals". Accelerationists hope this superintelligence will yield a "radical abundance". As Sam Altman once said, once general AI is invented, "poverty really does just end".

Andreessen, the author of a "techno-optimist manifesto", puts it his way: we are "poised for an intelligence takeoff that will expand our capabilities to unimagined heights". The technology, he claims, is "liberatory of human potential. Liberatory of the human soul, the human spirit. Expanding what it can mean to be free, to be fulfilled, to be alive." It follows, on this view, that slowing AI development is not merely unwise but immoral. "[A]ny deceleration of AI will cost lives. Deaths that were preventable by the AI that was prevented from existing is a form of murder." By comparison, at least, the way the Chinese government speaks about AI is more modest. Yes, China's economic leadership views AI as a priority and has boldly claimed it seeks to lead the world by 2030. Yet the rhetoric lacks the eschatological tone common in Silicon Valley. Chinese economic planners appear more interested in AI as a tool for industrial processes than as a means of creating a superintelligence that will reach the singularity. The State Council's 2025 "AI+" initiative is focused entirely on efficiency-enhancing applications rather than intelligence explosions. There is another important difference. China is banking far more heavily on simpler, lower-cost open-source AI models. In the US, most of the leading "frontier" AI models are secret and proprietary, in part as a business model and in part due to the apocryphal fears that the wrong actors could trigger human extinction. The smaller, lower-cost Chinese models may be seeking, in that sense, to be the more nimble 1970s Toyota rivals to the giant American cars produced by General Motors. More importantly, China is hedging its bets by investing heavily in a wide range of other technologies that might reasonably be described as "the future". In 2024, the country invested an estimated $940bn in clean-energy capex, broadly defined as renewables, electricity grids and energy storage (batteries), dwarfing its AI investments. In these sectors, AI is meant to be a complement -- the glue rather than the structure. While China's overall economy remains weaker than it was in the 2010s, elements of this broader strategy seem to be bearing fruit. Last year, 70 per cent of the world's EVs were manufactured in China. China also accounts for roughly 80-85 per cent of global solar photovoltaic manufacturing, and more than 75 per cent of all global battery production. If we set AI aside for a moment and assume that these technologies represent the future, it is clear who is ahead. The popular notion of an international AI race, and of a Chinese state gripped by existential obsession with AI, seems to come more from American sources than Chinese. In a 2020 paper, for example, political scientist Graham Allison and former Google chief executive Eric Schmidt argued that China desperately needs to develop AI to prevent the collapse of its state-planned system. "The command of 1.4 billion citizens by a Party-controlled authoritarian government is a herculean challenge," they wrote. "AI could give the Party . . . a claim to advance a model of governance -- a national operating system -- superior to today's dysfunctional democracies." In essence, the need for social control was said to leave China no choice but to invest heavily in AI. The theory is clever, but whether it is right is another question. What is clear is that the Party believes economic performance is essential to its survival, but, as described above, that conviction has led it towards a diversified strategy, not a single bet on artificial intelligence. Frontier AI, the most expensive form, has not been the main emphasis, and China appears to think it can do what it needs with "good enough" AI. The success of DeepSeek -- a decent domestic copy of ChatGPT -- may validate that strategy. The US-China "AI race" does serve another function: it is an excellent messaging and lobbying tool for the American tech industry. Schmidt, Mark Zuckerberg and others have insisted that government intervention such as antitrust enforcement would handicap the US in this end-of-times battle for civilisation itself. The idea of a race also justifies extraordinary levels of spending, lest someone else swoop in and take the prize.

It is probably more accurate to say that the real AI race is an American race that dates from the 2010s -- with China added to the narrative later. In the US, this contest moved from academia to commerce when Google began aggressively buying up AI talent and firms, including Britain's DeepMind and Geoffrey Hinton's DNNresearch. The race was joined in 2015 when Elon Musk and Sam Altman agreed on the need to fund non-profit OpenAI as a rival to Google. And that American race was from the beginning more eschatological than commercial. Ray Kurzweil in 2005 set 2045 as the date for the coming singularity; hired by Google, he in 2017 asserted that "we will multiply our effective intelligence a billion fold by merging with the intelligence we have created". Musk, meanwhile, became obsessed with the dangers that superintelligent AI "could render humanity extinct". From the beginning, extravagant, end-of-history rhetoric has been the hallmark of the field. Unlike in China, the American doubling and tripling down on AI has coincided with a move away from investment in clean-energy technologies and a reduction in support for basic research in other fields. The Trump administration has worked to de-incentivise electric vehicle uptake and clean-energy investment, and in response GM and Ford have reduced their investments in EVs and battery production. The US has further reduced spending on basic science, now at roughly one-third of its 1960s level, and has waged rhetorical war on major academic research universities such as Harvard and Columbia. With the federal government reducing its commitment to science and clean energy, the American bet on the future increasingly looks like artificial intelligence or bust. I would not deny the possibility that the accelerationists are right and that we may yet achieve an AGI that works for the betterment of humanity. If so, this period will be remembered for its brilliant grasp of what was needed to elevate humanity into a new and better age. But that outcome is far from certain, and both history and physics suggest caution. Moore's law -- the observation that the number of transistors on a chip doubles roughly every two years -- was an exception. For most technologies, after an initial inventive burst, the rate of progress begins to level off and further investment yields diminishing returns. The lightbulb was a giant improvement on the candle, but the next decade of innovation was comparatively incremental. Physics has a stubborn way of limiting exponential improvement: the speed of air travel, to take an obvious example, has not increased since the 1960s; and the efficiency of nuclear reactors, similarly, has improved only incrementally since the 1970s. Contemporary AI based on neural networks and deep learning has undoubtedly undergone extraordinary, disruptive improvement over the past decade. But over the next decade, progress may become more incremental, or run into new limits. Some constraints may arise from a scarcity of patterned data from which to learn, the sheer amount of energy required, or the computational challenges created by the physical world. The undeniable truth is that we simply do not know where we are on the curve -- and anyone who claims otherwise is making things up. The bet that AI will continue to improve exponentially is therefore uncertain, and the concentrated, monopolised nature of the current tech industry adds to the problem. Spending decisions have become highly centralised -- the decisions of just a few companies, in a manner more usually associated with state planning -- and groupthink is a real danger. The stock market, which has richly rewarded those investing in AI despite the vague business case, is not yet serving as any kind of check. Even if the all-in AI strategy is risky, might the spending still be defensible? One defence is cultural: Silicon Valley has a habit of going wildly overboard on new ideas, but even overshooting the mark may be better than being too cautious. Alternatively, it can be argued that spending on almost anything is preferable to hoarding profits or channelling them into stock buybacks. One estimate suggests that over the past 10 years the IT sector has spent more than $2.1tn on buybacks; arguably, spending that money on any genuine capital projects is better for the economy than spending to inflate share prices. From this point of view, the money could be used to build Mars rockets or elaborate amusement parks, so long as it is actually spent. Another defence of AI spending is more ominous: that the big talk is just that, and the real objective is for existing tech monopolies to build a deeper moat around their current businesses. Amazon's $2.45tn market capitalisation, to take one example, is a prize many would-be challengers would love to claim a part of, and AI could be the agent of industrial succession. On this view, Big Tech's AI splurge is meant to forestall a challenge from AI start-ups. That's a story that has much less to do with US-China competition than with the desire of powerful companies to entrench their position. It is, in other words, best understood less as an investment in humanity, and more as an investment in corporate immortality.

Over the past year, major US tech companies have spent more than $350bn on AI-related infrastructure, with projections of over $400bn for 2026. This far exceeds the spending of any other nation -- most notably China, where total investment is closer to an estimated $100bn. For many in the West, it may be reassuring that we have companies bold enough and capital markets deep enough to dominate a spending contest. If artificial intelligence is -- as prophesied -- the one ring to rule them all, then it would seem the west has the future in hand. That is the optimistic story. Yet there is another possibility: that Silicon Valley's obsession with AI could mean winning the AI race but losing a broader contest for economic pre-eminence. That follows because the US has gone all-in on AI, while China is spreading its bets across several plausible futures. It all depends on the bet on AI being the right one. Despite all the talk of an existential AI race, China is somewhat less committed to AI than sometimes portrayed. Beijing regularly describes AI as a "national strategic priority" and has invested to avoid falling too far behind. But the state and its major companies are spending much more money to secure dominance in other domains, such as electric vehicles, batteries, robotics, solar panels, wind turbines and other forms of advanced manufacturing. These sectors may be less glamorous, but their returns are far less speculative. It is the US that is truly infatuated with AI, with investments influenced by goals that are as mystical as they are commercial, especially the pursuit of Artificial General Intelligence and "the singularity". There is a strong belief in continued exponential progress -- a rarity in the history of technology. The deeper one digs, the more otherworldly it becomes, among both AI proponents and doomsayers. The concentrated, monopolised nature of the US tech sector adds to the risk: with so much spending power in so few hands, the danger of groupthink grows. What to do? If the pay-off from AI is uncertain, the prudent strategy would involve diversification and hedging. But American venture capital is as fixated on AI as the tech platforms themselves, and financial markets have rewarded the current course. The public sector could hedge, but the US -- under Trump -- has cut back support for clean-energy investment, leaving the national tech strategy looking like a large wager on a single horse. Today's AI spending, while impressive, is undeniably a frontloaded bet on one vision of the future. It may prove inspired and visionary. Or it could be remembered as an overdone fixation on a technology whose utility is narrower than advertised. Its failure to deliver could also be the trigger for a destabilising stock crash that leaves the west behind. Seen from some distance, the American tech strategy looks like a straightforward syllogism: AI is the most important technology of the 21st century; it is extremely expensive to build; therefore, whoever spends the most will be the dominant civilisation of the future. But any syllogism stands or falls with its premises. The key question is whether today's version of AI is in fact the most important path to prosperity and a better future. That turns out to be more of a leap of faith than many assume. The business case has long been somewhat vague, and on closer examination much of what Silicon Valley presents as the goals of AI has a distinctly fantastic flavour. "We believe Artificial Intelligence is our alchemy, our Philosopher's Stone," writes venture capitalist Marc Andreessen. He and Silicon Valley's other "accelerationists" believe achieving artificial intelligence is the paramount goal for humanity, a kind of secular rapture. Believers think AI can solve most of our problems: just last week, Google DeepMind's Demis Hassabis suggested AI could invent free renewable clean energy and discover the cure for most diseases; humanity is then "travelling to the stars and spreading consciousness to the galaxy". In some ways, the American AI race looks not unlike the race to build the grandest cathedrals in late medieval Europe. Then, the motives were a mixture of pride, economics and a sincere religious belief the structures would bring humanity closer to salvation. The byproduct was some extraordinary architecture -- but also financial ruin for cities such as Beauvais and Cologne, overwhelmed by their own ambition. Inside Silicon Valley, the finish line of the "race" is the achievement of AGI: the moment when machines acquire humanlike cognitive versatility. Believers argue that once AGI arrives, it will trigger further breakthroughs -- the "law of accelerating returns". A superintelligence may then reach "the singularity", a concept popularised by Ray Kurzweil and Vernor Vinge, ushering in a regime described by the latter as "as radically different from our human past as we humans are from the lower animals". Accelerationists hope this superintelligence will yield a "radical abundance". As Sam Altman once said, once general AI is invented, "poverty really does just end". Andreessen, the author of a "techno-optimist manifesto", puts it his way: we are "poised for an intelligence takeoff that will expand our capabilities to unimagined heights". The technology, he claims, is "liberatory of human potential. Liberatory of the human soul, the human spirit. Expanding what it can mean to be free, to be fulfilled, to be alive." It follows, on this view, that slowing AI development is not merely unwise but immoral. "[A]ny deceleration of AI will cost lives. Deaths that were preventable by the AI that was prevented from existing is a form of murder." By comparison, at least, the way the Chinese government speaks about AI is more modest. Yes, China's economic leadership views AI as a priority and has boldly claimed it seeks to lead the world by 2030. Yet the rhetoric lacks the eschatological tone common in Silicon Valley. Chinese economic planners appear more interested in AI as a tool for industrial processes than as a means of creating a superintelligence that will reach the singularity. The State Council's 2025 "AI+" initiative is focused entirely on efficiency-enhancing applications rather than intelligence explosions. There is another important difference. China is banking far more heavily on simpler, lower-cost open-source AI models. In the US, most of the leading "frontier" AI models are secret and proprietary, in part as a business model and in part due to the apocryphal fears that the wrong actors could trigger human extinction. The smaller, lower-cost Chinese models may be seeking, in that sense, to be the more nimble 1970s Toyota rivals to the giant American cars produced by General Motors. More importantly, China is hedging its bets by investing heavily in a wide range of other technologies that might reasonably be described as "the future". In 2024, the country invested an estimated $940bn in clean-energy capex, broadly defined as renewables, electricity grids and energy storage (batteries), dwarfing its AI investments. In these sectors, AI is meant to be a complement -- the glue rather than the structure. While China's overall economy remains weaker than it was in the 2010s, elements of this broader strategy seem to be bearing fruit. Last year, 70 per cent of the world's EVs were manufactured in China. China also accounts for roughly 80-85 per cent of global solar photovoltaic manufacturing, and more than 75 per cent of all global battery production. If we set AI aside for a moment and assume that these technologies represent the future, it is clear who is ahead. The popular notion of an international AI race, and of a Chinese state gripped by existential obsession with AI, seems to come more from American sources than Chinese. In a 2020 paper, for example, political scientist Graham Allison and former Google chief executive Eric Schmidt argued that China desperately needs to develop AI to prevent the collapse of its state-planned system. "The command of 1.4 billion citizens by a Party-controlled authoritarian government is a herculean challenge," they wrote. "AI could give the Party . . . a claim to advance a model of governance -- a national operating system -- superior to today's dysfunctional democracies." In essence, the need for social control was said to leave China no choice but to invest heavily in AI. The theory is clever, but whether it is right is another question. What is clear is that the Party believes economic performance is essential to its survival, but, as described above, that conviction has led it towards a diversified strategy, not a single bet on artificial intelligence. Frontier AI, the most expensive form, has not been the main emphasis, and China appears to think it can do what it needs with "good enough" AI. The success of DeepSeek -- a decent domestic copy of ChatGPT -- may validate that strategy. The US-China "AI race" does serve another function: it is an excellent messaging and lobbying tool for the American tech industry. Schmidt, Mark Zuckerberg and others have insisted that government intervention such as antitrust enforcement would handicap the US in this end-of-times battle for civilisation itself. The idea of a race also justifies extraordinary levels of spending, lest someone else swoop in and take the prize. It is probably more accurate to say that the real AI race is an American race that dates from the 2010s -- with China added to the narrative later. In the US, this contest moved from academia to commerce when Google began aggressively buying up AI talent and firms, including Britain's DeepMind and Geoffrey Hinton's DNNresearch. The race was joined in 2015 when Elon Musk and Sam Altman agreed on the need to fund non-profit OpenAI as a rival to Google. And that American race was from the beginning more eschatological than commercial. Ray Kurzweil in 2005 set 2045 as the date for the coming singularity; hired by Google, he in 2017 asserted that "we will multiply our effective intelligence a billion fold by merging with the intelligence we have created". Musk, meanwhile, became obsessed with the dangers that superintelligent AI "could render humanity extinct". From the beginning, extravagant, end-of-history rhetoric has been the hallmark of the field. Unlike in China, the American doubling and tripling down on AI has coincided with a move away from investment in clean-energy technologies and a reduction in support for basic research in other fields. The Trump administration has worked to de-incentivise electric vehicle uptake and clean-energy investment, and in response GM and Ford have reduced their investments in EVs and battery production. The US has further reduced spending on basic science, now at roughly one-third of its 1960s level, and has waged rhetorical war on major academic research universities such as Harvard and Columbia. With the federal government reducing its commitment to science and clean energy, the American bet on the future increasingly looks like artificial intelligence or bust. I would not deny the possibility that the accelerationists are right and that we may yet achieve an AGI that works for the betterment of humanity. If so, this period will be remembered for its brilliant grasp of what was needed to elevate humanity into a new and better age. But that outcome is far from certain, and both history and physics suggest caution. Moore's law -- the observation that the number of transistors on a chip doubles roughly every two years -- was an exception. For most technologies, after an initial inventive burst, the rate of progress begins to level off and further investment yields diminishing returns. The lightbulb was a giant improvement on the candle, but the next decade of innovation was comparatively incremental. Physics has a stubborn way of limiting exponential improvement: the speed of air travel, to take an obvious example, has not increased since the 1960s; and the efficiency of nuclear reactors, similarly, has improved only incrementally since the 1970s. Contemporary AI based on neural networks and deep learning has undoubtedly undergone extraordinary, disruptive improvement over the past decade. But over the next decade, progress may become more incremental, or run into new limits. Some constraints may arise from a scarcity of patterned data from which to learn, the sheer amount of energy required, or the computational challenges created by the physical world. The undeniable truth is that we simply do not know where we are on the curve -- and anyone who claims otherwise is making things up. The bet that AI will continue to improve exponentially is therefore uncertain, and the concentrated, monopolised nature of the current tech industry adds to the problem. Spending decisions have become highly centralised -- the decisions of just a few companies, in a manner more usually associated with state planning -- and groupthink is a real danger. The stock market, which has richly rewarded those investing in AI despite the vague business case, is not yet serving as any kind of check. Even if the all-in AI strategy is risky, might the spending still be defensible? One defence is cultural: Silicon Valley has a habit of going wildly overboard on new ideas, but even overshooting the mark may be better than being too cautious. Alternatively, it can be argued that spending on almost anything is preferable to hoarding profits or channelling them into stock buybacks. One estimate suggests that over the past 10 years the IT sector has spent more than $2.1tn on buybacks; arguably, spending that money on any genuine capital projects is better for the economy than spending to inflate share prices. From this point of view, the money could be used to build Mars rockets or elaborate amusement parks, so long as it is actually spent. Another defence of AI spending is more ominous: that the big talk is just that, and the real objective is for existing tech monopolies to build a deeper moat around their current businesses. Amazon's $2.45tn market capitalisation, to take one example, is a prize many would-be challengers would love to claim a part of, and AI could be the agent of industrial succession. On this view, Big Tech's AI splurge is meant to forestall a challenge from AI start-ups. That's a story that has much less to do with US-China competition than with the desire of powerful companies to entrench their position. It is, in other words, best understood less as an investment in humanity, and more as an investment in corporate immortality. | | The Antimonopolist (C) 2025 -

name: Mark Kelly
subject: Time to fight like hell
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
Don’t make the mistake of thinking this is just about me. It’s about every person who has the courage to speak out against Trump. Trump wants all of us living in fear, and I don’t intend to let him win.
#

Team: I’m writing to ask you for $5.
That’s right: just $5.
$5 because we’re under enormous pressure right now.
$5 because Donald Trump wants to stamp out any and all opposition, and he’s willing to undermine your First Amendment rights to do it.
$5 because he’s sent his lapdog, Pete Hegseth, after me, and he’s moving ahead with a sham investigation to drag my name through the mud and destroy everything I fought for during my 25 years in uniform.
Don’t make the mistake of thinking this is just about me. It’s about every person who has the courage to speak out against Trump. Trump wants all of us living in fear, and I don’t intend to let him win.
It’s time to fight like hell. I need your help to do it.
[**Can you make a contribution of $5, or whatever you can afford, to help power our work against Trump, Hegseth, and anyone else who would take away our liberties?**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-dec25-dod/.FHtwNx&refcode=em251219-1-1m&t=1&akid=6304%2E6688874%2EC1o4DN)

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=6304%2E6688874%2EC1o4DN).

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Corporate special interests will spend millions against me
body:
---
Derek, it’s Sherrod Brown. **Today, I’m going to ask you to[**split a donation between my Senate campaign in Ohio and the DNC.**](https://ak.democrats.org/go/29244?refcode=em_20251219_5567_fr&amount=20&t=1&akid=5567%2E22930333%2ESldvdj)**But first, I want to explain why your support is so important and what could happen without it.** Here’s what’s going on in Ohio:
* Experts at _The Cook Political Report_ have increased our chances of winning.
* I’ve been travelling all across the state to meet with voters directly, listen to their stories, and build momentum on the ground.
* Not one but two polls now show me leading my Republican opponent, Jon Husted.

Folks in Ohio and across our country are hurting, Derek — and Republicans like Husted aren’t lifting a finger to help. Husted _continues_ to put the interests of his billionaire friends and corporate special interest allies first. It’s shameful, and that’s why we have to defeat him. **I’m counting on folks like you to keep our grassroots movement going strong.**[**Corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me, and the only way I can fight back and win is with your support. Will you split a contribution between my campaign and the DNC to help?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-brown-split?refcode=em_20251219_5567_fr&amount=20&refcode2=5567_22930333_Sldvdj&akid=5567%2E22930333%2ESldvdj) Your donation today won’t just support our fight in Ohio — it will power the critical work that the DNC is doing nationwide to win up and down the ballot. And with our most important fundraising deadline of the year coming up on December 31, right now is when your contribution will have the biggest impact. After the month ends, our numbers go public. We’ll be required to report how much we’ve raised over the past few months and how many donations we’ve received. **Those numbers will tell a story — everyone will be waiting to see whether or not we have what it takes to flip Ohio’s Senate seat.** It’s important we make it crystal clear: _We’re not done fighting. ---

President Donald Trump announced the launch of Freedom 250 to celebrate the nation's 250th birthday in 2026, with plans for **[the"first-ever Patriot Games"](https://substack.com/redirect/fd0044f1-28f0-4000-b208-adf65dfd7842?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**--a four-day athletic event featuring "one young man and one young woman from each state and territory" competing next fall, along with other festivities including a "Great American State Fair" on the National Mall (June 25-July 10), Independence Day celebrations, and a UFC event at the White House on June 14.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/fd0044f1-28f0-4000-b208-adf65dfd7842?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[](https://substack.com/redirect/a1bb617f-fb71-4768-b6f5-41a9af6bd584?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Announcement draws "Hunger Games" comparisons from critics:** Trump's video announcement included a promise that "there will be no men playing in women's sports," nodding to his administration's efforts to prohibit transgender athletes from women's sports, while the "Patriot Games" concept prompted social media comparisons to "The Hunger Games" fictional death matches, with Democratic governors JB Pritzker and **[Gavin Newsom posting memes](https://substack.com/redirect/d1371ccb-a41b-4a34-a2c7-d4d0be85b411?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** and clips from the dystopian movie series.

* * *
**[Together with Health Sciences Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/96c3ce7b-d922-4247-8253-2a9f7a342c59?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

# **Brown University shooting suspect identified as foreign national and former physics student**
Authorities identified Claudio Manuel Neves-Valente, 48, a Portuguese national who studied physics at Brown from fall 2000 to spring 2001 before withdrawing in 2003, **[as the suspect in Saturday's mass shooting](https://substack.com/redirect/e3236387-c900-4f68-b27e-b5526e59095e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that killed two Brown students--Ella Cook and Mukhammad Aziz Umurzokov--and injured nine during a finals week review session, as well as the Monday murder of renowned MIT nuclear physics professor Nuno Loureiro at his Massachusetts home.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e3236387-c900-4f68-b27e-b5526e59095e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
FBI Boston
* **Found dead from self-inflicted gunshot in New Hampshire storage unit:** Federal agents found Neves-Valente dead from a self-inflicted gunshot wound Thursday evening after breaching a storage locker in Salem, New Hampshire, with law enforcement in tactical gear having been outside the unit for hours, ending a days-long manhunt that involved canvassing neighborhoods for surveillance video showing a masked, stocky figure around 5 feet, 8 inches tall walking with an odd gait.
* **Connection to MIT victim through Portuguese university:** Neves-Valente had been terminated from a monitor position at Instituto Superior Tecnico in Portugal in 2000--the same university attended by MIT victim Nuno Loureiro--with Brown President Christina Paxson noting the suspect "spent a great deal of time" in the Barus and Holley Building where the shooting occurred during his time as a Ph.D. student in physics, though his motive remains unclear.

* * *
# **Homeless hero cracks Brown/MIT shooting case wide open - Entitled to $50,000 reward**
A homeless tipster known only as "John," who had been living in the basement of Brown's engineering building, is **[credited with cracking the Brown University and MIT shootings case](https://substack.com/redirect/90f7bd50-d79e-4ad4-a9f2-6b5de439ce30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after providing key information about suspect Claudio Neves Valente's car--a grey Nissan with Florida plates--following an odd encounter, with Rhode Island Attorney General Peter Neronha saying "He blew this case right open."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/90f7bd50-d79e-4ad4-a9f2-6b5de439ce30?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **John entitled to $50,000 reward for crucial tip:** FBI Special Agent Ted Docks said John should be eligible for the staggering $50,000 reward after his Reddit post and subsequent interview with investigators proved crucial to identifying the 48-year-old suspect, with John noting he found it odd that Neves Valente approached his car with a key fob, backed away, and then circled the block, prompting John to follow him.
* **Strange "cat and mouse" encounter hours before shooting:** John told investigators he had a bizarre interaction with Neves Valente in the bathroom of Brown's engineering building just hours before the attack, noting the suspect's clothing was "inappropriate and inadequate for the weather," and later yelled "Your car is back there, why are you circling the block?" to which the suspect responded "I don't know you from nobody" and repeatedly asked "Why are you harassing me?"

* * *
# WATCH: Erika Kirk says her mission is to get JD Vance elected President
Erika Kirk announced at TPUSA's America Fest event in Phoenix, AZ that she seeks to get Vice President JD Vance elected as President in 2028.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d0e4e929-f94e-4173-9e04-acdfeecb3d6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **Illegal immigrant released by Fairfax County, allegedly killed man next day**
Marvin Morales-Ortez, a 23-year-old illegal immigrant from El Salvador and suspected MS-13 member with **[at least seven criminal charges](https://substack.com/redirect/bf910bcd-eb28-4229-b1f3-c470887ddb3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** in Fairfax County, was released Tuesday after the George Soros-backed Commonwealth's Attorney's Office dropped malicious wounding and firearm charges, then allegedly shot and killed a man in his Northern Virginia home Wednesday following a manhunt.
* **Trump administration blames "sanctuary politicians" with "blood on their hands":** A Homeland Security spokesperson slammed Fairfax County officials, stating "sanctuary politicians have blood on their hands" and "should have turned this serial criminal illegal alien over to ICE law enforcement--instead they chose to RELEASE him back into Virginia neighborhoods," while the Commonwealth's Attorney's Office claimed charges were dropped due to insufficient evidence after the victim moved out of the country and refused to cooperate.
* **Sheriff 's office says ICE declined to seek judicial warrant:** The Fairfax County Sheriff's Office, led by Stacey Kincaid (currently being considered by Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger for a top Virginia State Police position), said "ICE was aware of Morales Ortez's incarceration and elected not to seek a judicial warrant to ensure he remained in custody," though DHS countered that "to honor a detainer, officials do NOT need a judicial warrant."

* * *

name: Eva Piatek, Evergreen
subject: TL;DR – How we win, together
body:
Building a durable, affordable clean energy future
| | |
---
| Hi—

Did you see Beth’s note? I know you’re busy so here’s the TL;DR:Evergreen isn’t waiting for changes in Washington to keep pushing for real climate progress today. We’re working with newly-elected governors, providing data-backed playbooks to lower costs for families AND deploy clean energy at the state level.

But Big Oil and other special interest groups are already on the ground, trying to block these cost-saving measures. **Your generous donation helps us build research and policies that we can deliver straight into the hands of governors** —who can turn them into laws that make life more affordable for Americans AND take on the climate crisis.

[**Better yet, a generous donor has pledged a double match—meaning every dollar you give goes twice as far, up to $50,000. $50 becomes $100, $100 becomes $200, and so on.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376648/87SdQ4wZ)

This is how we build a durable clean energy future that no president can undo: by making clean energy the cornerstone of a thriving, affordable economy in every state around the country. **Thank you for your generous donation.** |
---
[GIVE NOW](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376649/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| Sincerely,

Eva Piatek
Digital Lead - Email
Evergreen Collaborative

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Beth Poole, Evergreen Collaborative
Date: Tue, Dec 16, 2025
Subject: Let us explain: A winning strategy |
| [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376650/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Hi Friend,

While the Trump regime continues to dismantle federal climate progress, Evergreen isn’t sitting back and waiting for changes in Washington.

We’re pushing for progress where it’s possible–right _now._ That’s always been our North Star: Championing the most ambitious policies that are politically possible at this moment.

The real battle for clean energy has shifted to the states—**and a generous donor has stepped up with a**[**$50,000 matching challenge**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376651/87SdQ4wZ)**, doubling every dollar donated by****December 31****to accelerate a proven strategy that’s winning today.** Let me explain.

Families across the country are feeling the pinch of rising costs. For too long, they’ve been told that clean energy is expensive.**Evergreen is flipping that script.** We are proving that the path to lower bills runs directly through state-level clean energy deployment.

**This isn’t just rhetoric; it’s a winning strategy.**

This year, we rapidly put together key policies for newly elected governors like Mikie Sherrill in New Jersey and Abigail Spanberger in Virginia, who both secured decisive victories by campaigning on energy affordability.

As they head into office in 2026, **we are handing them the specific, data-backed playbooks to deliver on those promises.**

This is the innovative, results-oriented work that separates us from the pack: we aren't just asking for climate action; we are building the exact policies that will make life more affordable for working families.

[**We need your help to turn these plans into reality in New Jersey, Virginia, and beyond.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376652/87SdQ4wZ) Fossil fuel interests are already working to block these cost-saving measures, and with the federal government stepping back, the pressure on states to lead has never been higher.

We have the data, but we need resources to build momentum and support for policies we are delivering to leaders. To help us meet this moment, a generous donor has agreed to match every dollar you give, **up to $50,000, before December 31.**

Please, don’t wait. This is your chance to drive climate progress that produces real financial relief for American families, defying the gridlock in Washington. [**Donate $25, $50, or whatever you can today to double your impact and help us build an affordable clean energy future.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376653/87SdQ4wZ) |
---
[MAKE A YEAR-END GIFT](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376654/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| With gratitude, |
---
| | |
---
### Beth Poole
Senior Fundraising Director
Evergreen Collaborative
---
---
* * *
---
| _Evergreen is building the ambitious, actionable policy roadmap for an all-out mobilization to defeat climate change—and to create millions of jobs in a thriving, just, and inclusive clean energy future. None of this would be possible without your support._ |
---
---
| | [ ](https://evergreenaction.com/?utm_source=digital_template&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=evergreen&utm_content=footer)
---
Follow Evergreen on Bluesky and LinkedIn.
---
[ ](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376655/87SdQ4wZ) | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376656/87SdQ4wZ)
---|---
And follow our new project, **Hope This Helps.**
---
[ ](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376657/87SdQ4wZ) | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376658/87SdQ4wZ) | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376659/87SdQ4wZ) | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4376660/87SdQ4wZ)
---|---|---|---

name: Roy Cooper
subject: I won’t be quiet when our health care is on the line
body:
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
Peter, I will stand up to the GOP to deliver for our country. I had to do just that to get Medicaid Expansion passed for North Carolina. So I’m not afraid to stand up against what’s happening now: **Republicans in Washington said they wouldn’t try to cut Medicaid. They lied — and we can’t let them get away with it.** **[Add your name to our petition and call on GOP lawmakers to protect health care like they promised.](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_Medicaid_102625?source=em_RC_PET_MedicaidRESEND_121925&t=7&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)** Republicans are trying to take health care away from millions of Americans to fund a tax break for their billionaire donors. It doesn’t get more absurd than that. No American should go without health care. Not in the richest country in the world. That’s why I worked so hard as Governor to get Medicaid Expansion done — and now more than 675,000 more North Carolinians are covered. I was proud to work to achieve a bipartisan deal for NC. Now, Washington Republicans need to come to the table, too. **[If you agree, please sign my petition to demand the GOP end this attack on our health care and protect Medicaid like they promised.](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_Medicaid_102625?t=8&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)** | **[SIGN ON](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_Medicaid_102625?source=em_RC_PET_MedicaidRESEND_121925&t=9&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)**
---
Thank you for standing with me,
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_Medicaid_102625?source=em_RC_PET_MedicaidRESEND_121925&t=10&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?source=&t=4&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=11&akid=193%2E124072%2Ero3XU_)

body:
If MAGA takes control of the state Supreme Courts in the critical swings of Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan in 2026, Trump will be handed a lethal weapon that could deal a fatal blow to democracy.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oV7ht4S_Jy9JDkp-xQk_ALLukBDqqbOkqrSCzrjW1tcehdNz1P69FM1N8OjAur0PoCiflPCuyuXhmRdfRSmccp35s5A488by3TdSgFd--c_d_UOGSp5FWBOhC9IHidq-6mZWDmZrecGVEYWboHQFZgrZW51o-LqAZNmJvtwxoL3y-C4CwC_oBy2LOM6tog9Li7jZSHKfHxWbF6NAlYOrzpp8R2RF8TdgiQlMaeUY9yI4tOjJM5Dhug==&c=gV_4GN2-KkIuh_kbHiZXIL8XqSfPYNwxafFgcfxBDDitb2e7BcG7dw==&ch=B0aLF3Cy_bKCA_26fgGy64f8bdQpbieJE1NH5EyJoAeUSqTtU3UQiQ==)
---
| Peter,
A bombshell report just dropped from the New York Times.
In 2020, Donald Trump called the Speaker of the Georgia House and demanded he call a special legislative session to nullify the state's presidential election results.
Leaked audio reveals Trump asked the Speaker: "Who's gonna stop you?"
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001oV7ht4S_Jy9JDkp-xQk_ALLukBDqqbOkqrSCzrjW1tcehdNz1P69FM1N8OjAur0Pbtyy5AxCipC3JRc320ZN6DJNun2yJ2z4KRiuOGpYcJWjDKYrXiu2P-HJi8172hzOai9BW1TYPQgDyK1cZSBnX7FINEGpJNwBrTGotU1p3QOBxWaZynbKZMI70rontZwQ3_dn6BzdRXa95XIjAbE8glmzMphbPlpubTcqad4OyWqC8FBnTTZgxg==&c=gV_4GN2-KkIuh_kbHiZXIL8XqSfPYNwxafFgcfxBDDitb2e7BcG7dw==&ch=B0aLF3Cy_bKCA_26fgGy64f8bdQpbieJE1NH5EyJoAeUSqTtU3UQiQ==)
---

We know the answer. If the Speaker had caved to Trump's demands, the only people left to stand in the way of Trump's election rigging scheme were the judges on GA's State Supreme Court. State Supreme Courts have final word on election laws.
That is what is at stake in 2026.
If MAGA takes control of the State Supreme Courts in the critical swings of Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan in 2026, Trump will be handed a lethal weapon with which he can deal the fatal blow to our democracy.
Just this week, it was reported that Trump is plotting a third term with Alan Dershowitz, Epstein's former lawyer, and billionaire megadonor Miriam Adelson promised a $250 million check to bankroll the effort.
Our courts are the only thing standing in the way.
We must ensure Trump never gets that lethal weapon. Defend Our Courts is once again leading the defense for Democrats, but we need your help if we have any hope of holding MAGA off in three states at once.

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |

name: Voter Registration Team @ Voter Action Project
subject: Riding 2025's momentum
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
The 2025 elections showed us a valuable truth, folks: Voter participation is increasing in all the right ways.
Voter registration activity increased across the country. According to the team at National Voter Registration Day, New York recorded more registrations for their 2025 events than in 2024, and California saw similar increases. Earlier this fall it looked like voter participation was going to make a real jump, even in a so-called "off-year".
Then Election Day came. New Jersey had more voters turn out for a gubernatorial election than they had in 20 years. Crucial counties in Pennsylvania saw their highest turnout levels in almost 15 years. New York City reached a turnout percentage unseen in over five decades.
We can inspire even higher levels of participation when voters understand the stakes and feel like their vote matters, just like it did a few weeks ago. That's why our voter registration efforts are so important.
Just $3 can help us register a new voter. Will you step up with $24 or more today to help register 8 new voters? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?refcode=121925_1&amount=24]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121925_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121925_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121925_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121925_1&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?express_lane=true&refcode=121925_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?refcode=121925_1&amount=36]
The key is getting young people -- a major target of opponents of voting rights across the country -- to see the importance of voting now more than ever. Pundits in Virginia said they could have called the election results when they saw the first turnout numbers from the major college campus locations around the state. That's the kind of impact these young voters can have.
Voting quickly becomes a habit -- a habit our democracy sorely needs to fulfill the promise of the American dream. Overwhelming key states with new voter registrations can help turn the tide against the voter suppression laws we've seen passed the last few years.
The 2026 midterm election will determine whether our democracy stays mired in the darkness and conspiracy theories we hear every day or if we can reclaim our rights -- from voting to choice and more -- and march forward to a better future.
New voter registration -- at a cost to us of just about $3 per new voter -- can be game-changing. Can you pitch in with $24 or however much you can to help us register a tidal wave of new voters ahead of next year's voter registration deadlines? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-voterregistration?refcode=121925_1&amount=24]
Thank you,
Voter Action Project's Registration Team
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121925_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121925_1&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.

name: Trisha C.
subject: Why I went on a conservative talk radio station as a Democrat
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
Boebert is too busy embarrassing us in D.C., attending parties for felon George Santos, and fighting with Marjorie Taylor Greene to show up.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vdD0hr5UiabckCRCDUZqrtCk5XaPVd1HVYx_waW5P-mLCuyJECP7Ca_ySdnVtxKycOfMbxTpYMVUOe7z2EwNMGDFZ14SnK57E_brOAqAGbuwNR9-JSHoaB6o_QTu9T8a26Au2P5X6A0bNd7wdqPlOHNi4R37Peu7C4-XbB4yUm_8se5sB2AkFb9GoIUgfJd7M6ZHQepw7SrwjxUzwsNg7pPAA56e86sb6Wk0AklWTe8=&c=K-AVUift3FAkLwCl3SadDBvtsY7rygpPQ2PdwLK2qv1Bn1GMXbCjjg==&ch=4qStKY37gxyGgeoiIWaJwBrBCL2c3xgZRgHJmdkXGoEe2JKVQH_nKA==)
---
|

Peter,
Where is Lauren Boebert?
That's the question I have been asking--and have been getting asked--on conservative radio in my district.
You might think that talking on conservative media is a waste of time, but that would be an incorrect assumption. Just as in 2024, Democrats need to think outside the box and show up in places we don't normally participate to reach less-political people with our economic reform/anti-corruption message.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001vdD0hr5UiabckCRCDUZqrtCk5XaPVd1HVYx_waW5P-mLCuyJECP7Ca_ySdnVtxKycOfMbxTpYMVUOe7z2EwNMGDFZ14SnK57E_brOAqAGbuwNR9-JSHoaB6o_QTu9T8a26Au2P5X6A0bNd7wdqPlOHNi4R37Peu7C4-XbB4yUm_8se5sB2AkFb9GoIUgfJd7M6ZHQepw7SrwjxUzwsNg7pPAA56e86sbu6sSJaw0sH4=&c=K-AVUift3FAkLwCl3SadDBvtsY7rygpPQ2PdwLK2qv1Bn1GMXbCjjg==&ch=4qStKY37gxyGgeoiIWaJwBrBCL2c3xgZRgHJmdkXGoEe2JKVQH_nKA==)
---

But none of this comes easy. This is the result of months of hard work from me and my team, doing the outreach, laying the groundwork, and reaching people outside of the mainstream media.
Which brings me to the central question I get in every interview: where is Boebert?
The only public outreach Boebert does is puff pieces on MAGA shows like Russia propagandist Tim Pool or NewsNation - but never shows up on local media to talk policy.
All the while, people here need help from our member of Congress, and she is instead too busy embarrassing us in D.C., attending parties for felon George Santos, or using campaign funds to attend Kid Rock concerts.

clean_body: Boebert is too busy embarrassing us in D.C., attending parties for felon George Santos, and fighting with Marjorie Taylor Greene to show up. | | | | | == | Peter, Where is Lauren Boebert? That's the question I have been asking--and have been getting asked--on conservative radio in my district. You might think that talking on conservative media is a waste of time, but that would be an incorrect assumption. Just as in 2024, Democrats need to think outside the box and show up in places we don't normally participate to reach less-political people with our economic reform/anti-corruption message. | == But none of this comes easy. This is the result of months of hard work from me and my team, doing the outreach, laying the groundwork, and reaching people outside of the mainstream media. Which brings me to the central question I get in every interview: where is Boebert? The only public outreach Boebert does is puff pieces on MAGA shows like Russia propagandist Tim Pool or NewsNation - but never shows up on local media to talk policy. All the while, people here need help from our member of Congress, and she is instead too busy embarrassing us in D.C., attending parties for felon George Santos, or using campaign funds to attend Kid Rock concerts. Boebert has got to go. We can fire her for good. Boebert is only in Congress because of a 546 vote margin. In 2024, as her Democratic opponent, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts as Boebert underperformed Trump in every single county. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | == Thank you, Trisha Calvarese | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121604300/589992244/-629675555?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjIzNjQzMzQtZjVkYy1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=DJUm-NnJpbKiOtHRJL73LjDVG_00WKoPOZb26jRSahQ=) I’ll be honest with you: I didn’t run for Congress to play politics. **I ran for Congress because, like many of you, I want my children to inherit a better world than the one I grew up in.** That’s why I’ve fought hard to strengthen public education, create good-paying jobs, and make housing more affordable. **I ran for Congress because I believe all hardworking American families deserve the opportunity to get ahead.** That’s why I’ve made it my number one priority in the House to bring down costs, protect affordable healthcare, and make life easier for working families. **I ran for Congress because Oregonians deserve a leader who will always show up for us.** That’s why I’ve held over 18 town halls and met with over 42,000 constituents since taking office, and I’m committed to standing up for the needs of our families and communities in Washington. **I ran for Congress because the people of OR-05 deserve better than the GOP’s failed leadership.** That’s why I’m determined to defend this seat, take back the House in 2026, and get to work fighting for the issues that matter most to Oregonians.
