name: Roy Cooper
subject: This is the work of a lifetime
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Foushee for Congress
body:
I have a humble request to make today.

If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/9_07fM1dnEmqdVR1wv8kcQ2?unsubscribedata=mOAGyrRKRCXNYQmlNhcNjwh%2B4Yd0u1DpO4qay39pRJHUIN8MYOhCa3%2Fp3V%2FTcER1blLKS4P5PVpZEMRdeJYvQlsHom7SeT8qent7%2FFbBDKKQSMPhTCvKeEOZPI0hDSpOSTvf3hUcEHQrOkAE3XwUTmbG%2BX4cnnTuaTijjPeTINzqf4oQhBLlD06S1bsedNlO%2FUqHo%2FDeRjuc2VmcLj1hXHrWhLBKvn92n1fgshk9eiRuZY0efMcRtYj4JF0ln3vdOJfByyIK0ziQMlGqZ2s2zIEfBgR7QGAC4Za%2FdM83Vstdq8w9u4mugeX925a76zyRl7ILFQ7Zi5bvPtkCYQJa3b7l4KDerSTJHprWasbUumfAiQmhLhwVWK9mFdrsNHcL9GzT0uoXN%2ByRVfq%2Fphy8SZ4F6mfTeLoYfbtiTq8rBc4%3D)
---
|
Peter, I have spent my working life trying to make North Carolina better, and it has been more than worth it. **[Now, I am running for the U.S. Senate to continue that work, but our toss-up race is getting closer, and I need your help to win. Split a donation between our campaign and Valerie Foushee today to say you’re with me.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121612361/590017921/1634711932?amount=10&source=SBD_VF_EM_SPL_121925_COOPER_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMjkxZTFlZi0wMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ODAjrhWR41wNw0BDrRwSXgPyGO3CcKJHM4bPQgZfloM=)** I’m proud of what we have achieved together: expanding Medicaid. Getting raises for public school teachers. Investing in our clean energy economy. The trust that North Carolinians have placed in me has meant so much. It gave me the opportunity to serve my home and allowed us to make real progress. **I didn’t plan to go to Washington, but I got in this race for the U.S. Senate because that progress is on the line, along with so much more. For our state and for all Americans.** I will not sit by and watch while extreme politicians tear away the rights that so many fought for so long to secure. I will do everything in my power to protect our state. **I know that this is the work of a lifetime. ** And it is work that I cannot do alone, so I’m humbly asking you to stand with me. **[Peter, I am asking you to invest in our work by splitting a contribution between our campaign and Valerie Foushee today. National Republicans are rallying around my far-right opponent to try to defeat me, and I can’t overcome them alone. We can’t let Michael Whatley gain any more ground.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121612362/590017922/1634711932?amount=10&source=SBD_VF_EM_SPL_121925_COOPER_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMjkxZTFlZi0wMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ODAjrhWR41wNw0BDrRwSXgPyGO3CcKJHM4bPQgZfloM=)** I know that we can win together, just like we have before. clean_body: I have a humble request to make today. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | Peter, I have spent my working life trying to make North Carolina better, and it has been more than worth it. = I’m proud of what we have achieved together: expanding Medicaid. Getting raises for public school teachers. Investing in our clean energy economy. The trust that North Carolinians have placed in me has meant so much. It gave me the opportunity to serve my home and allowed us to make real progress. I didn’t plan to go to Washington, but I got in this race for the U.S. Senate because that progress is on the line, along with so much more. For our state and for all Americans. I will not sit by and watch while extreme politicians tear away the rights that so many fought for so long to secure. I will do everything in my power to protect our state. I know that this is the work of a lifetime. And it is work that I cannot do alone, so I’m humbly asking you to stand with me. = I know that we can win together, just like we have before. Gratefully, Roy Cooper * | | | ---|---|--- = Valerie is fighting to protect access to health care, working families, and to reform our criminal justice system. But she needs your help to continue to do this great work in Congress. Your donation right now will help protect Democrat Valerie Foushee by giving us a jumpstart on the next cycle. | = = Paid for by Foushee for Congress Valerie Foushee for Congress PO Box 16446 Chapel Hill, NC 27516 United States If you need to you can unsubscribe, You can also =.

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1145021&post_id=182102990&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgyMTAyOTkwLCJpYXQiOjE3NjYxNjY1MjksImV4cCI6MTc2ODc1ODUyOSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTExNDUwMjEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jqsK8ZTIe1nsawUwg3d7dvVilRb4Dh2PDw3wyuf6Iok)
---
| | [](https://substack..pQg5HFh3UjOy7PI3RG8XnjY0BpDkxu27YdMncuRfH9s?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/jeffjacksonnc/p/good-news-big-win?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Big win for our state to tell you about.
Here's what happened:
Back in July, I stood at a sewage pump station in Hillsborough that had just flooded during a tropical storm. Millions of gallons of sewage spilled into the river.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0f2a7aa9-c199-4c4e-a14f-6f9666b7b38a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
What made this especially frustrating was that this same station had already been approved by FEMA for funding to fix the problem. FEMA had selected it for a grant to move the station up a hill so it wouldn't flood again.
Then FEMA canceled the money.
And this wasn't an isolated case. FEMA had pulled the plug on 60 water and sewer projects across North Carolina - projects it had _already approved._ Overnight, our state was set to lose $200 million in infrastructure funding.
So we took FEMA to court - **and we just won.**
Our argument was straightforward: this wasn't FEMA's money to cancel. When Congress gave FEMA these funds, it was very clear the funds had to be spent on water and sewer projects in disaster-prone areas. Congress basically said, "Here's money for a specific program that we want you to administer." That meant FEMA didn't have the authority to cancel the program entirely, which is what it did.
The court agreed with us - strongly. So strongly, in fact, that we won at the summary judgment level.
What does that mean?
It means the court essentially said, "Before we even go to trial, the evidence here is so strong that we believe there's no way FEMA can prove that it didn't break the law, so we're making a decision without having to go through a full trial - and FEMA needs to pay what it owes."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9efa30aa-af0e-45a2-8022-b184abf7b58a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A huge thanks to our team at NCDOJ who took this case on and delivered a result that will make a difference for dozens of communities. A big win to lift our spirits as we head into the holidays.
### **Anthrax Donuts**
While our FEMA lawsuit was pending, I visited some of the towns whose projects had been abruptly canceled to see the impact on the ground.
One of them was the town of Whiteville, population 5,000.
A lot of people came out to greet me. The creek behind me in this photo floods on a regular basis, damaging homes and businesses.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/04016237-53ac-42c9-8173-7523349bc667?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is a long-standing issue for the town and they wanted me to know how important it is to them.
This included a very nice woman who owns a local donut shop, which floods on occasion. The last flood was just in October, and she told me she lost her whole inventory.
She very kindly brought me a box of donuts.
As she handed it to me, one of my staff members immediately swooped in and grabbed the box out of my hands. That struck me as odd, so I made a mental note to ask about it later.
Here's how that conversation went back at the car:
Me: "Why'd you grab the box?"
Staff: "I didn't know what was in it!"
Me: "It was a box of donuts. She was the donut lady."
Staff: "Well, it was a security issue."
Me: "It was a donut issue. I want my donuts back."
Staff: "Well, we don't know what's _in_ the donuts."
Me: "What could be in the donuts?"
Staff: "I don't know. Anthrax."
Me: "You think those are _anthrax donuts_?"
Staff: "They could be!"
So here's what happened:
We put the donuts on a table in our office until we could resolve the situation, and before I knew it, all of them were gone. Somehow, the same staff who was concerned these donuts might contain anthrax also decided they might be delicious, and promptly consumed them all.
And for the record, I am sending a check to the nice lady for the donuts. She's had a hard couple of months, and she doesn't need to give me anything for free.
Even if I didn't get to eat them.
### **In Appreciation of Gov. Jim Hunt**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d77d5898-f64d-47cb-8263-7f36278d2530?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The first time I met Governor Jim Hunt, I made a joke about how I had once been a very low-ranking soldier.
He immediately grabbed me by the wrist and said, firmly, "And that's who you're fighting for, Jeff - people who are on the bottom, who are trying to move up!"
I've never forgotten that moment, because it captured him perfectly. He believed public service meant looking out for the little guy, finding ways to move everyone up, and leading with compassion.
That same spirit showed up in personal ways, too. I never saw him happier than when he was campaigning for his daughter, Rachel. He was her star volunteer. I once saw him tell a voter, "Yes, it's really me - now you need to vote for my daughter!" I know she made him incredibly proud with her public service.
And even after he'd done everything he'd done - after all the offices, all the accomplishments - he kept doing the small, human things that make leadership real. He used to cut out newspaper clippings and send them to me. Most were about early childhood education - one of his central legacies as Governor. I always read them, and I always showed them to my wife, just to let her know I was pen pals with the Governor.
In the coming days, it's going to be difficult to summarize everything he accomplished for our state, everything he meant to people.
But it isn't going to be hard to sum him up as a person:
He was simply a great joy to be around, and he really cared about people, and he had a vision for ways he could bring people together to do big things that would serve them well.
When that's your character and you're given the chance to serve again and again, the impact compounds and the good you can do for people lasts for generations.
We're grateful - and we'll miss him.
### **Time with Family**
For the first time, Marisa and I are hosting our families for the holidays. Our house is about three days away from being absolutely full, with kids running around everywhere. We've got a trampoline in the backyard that's about to get a lot of use.
I hope you get to spend time with your loved ones as well, and that this holiday gives you the space to relax, unwind, and not talk politics unless that's what makes you happy.
And remember: it's ok to take a break from the news, just as it's ok - even good! - to take a break from work.
Wishing you all a wonderful holiday.
Best,
Jeff Jackson

body:
The Governor isn’t letting these lies and ugly politics get in the way of doing his job delivering progress to Minnesota families.
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251219-319&t=1&akid=319%2E34582%2E2NbS3N)
Did you see this, Peter?
[](https://act.timwalz.com/go/213?t=2&akid=319%2E34582%2E2NbS3N)
Secretary of Education (with no background in education) Linda McMahon is lobbing baseless attacks at Tim and calling on him to resign as Governor.
Donald Trump, his cabinet, and the MAGA movement are absolutely relentless in their misinformation war against Tim.
WWE millionaire McMahon posted her letter and called the Governor out on Twitter, probably to distract from the Trump administration’s plunging poll numbers.
Tim isn’t letting these lies and ugly politics get in the way of doing his job delivering progress for Minnesota families — but **we need to ensure our campaign is ready for all the far-right attacks to come in 2026**.
**[While Tim is focused on the work at hand here in the North Star State, can he count on you to pitch in $5 to ensure we reach our $100,000 goal before the December 31st public deadline?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251219.x9FdDz&amount=5&refcode=em20251219-319&t=3&akid=319%2E34582%2E2NbS3N)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251219.x9FdDz&amount=5&refcode=20251219-319&t=22&akid=319%2E34582%2E2NbS3N)**
Anything you can contribute will directly fuel our work to reelect Tim and keep Minnesota on a path of progress.
— Team Walz

**[Donate ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251219-319&t=23&refcode2=319_34582_2NbS3N&akid=319%2E34582%2E2NbS3N)**

body:
Hoping you can step up before midnight

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121612823/590019281/1328794144?refcode=ea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ms=ea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzY2Y4MDJlLTAyZGQtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMGQzYTFlYzhjNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=EJSFsKyLBEujHn1iI4bcvqKQ3xAn_xmyo65q_o6Lv7w=&emci=bdee29cf-fcdc-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4823105)

We’re bumping this to the top of your inbox because we’re counting on this team sending 1,000 more letters to Congress, and we’re hoping you can step up before midnight tonight. **_Trump’s EPA just sided with fossil fuel companies by walking away from a clean-air rule that scientists say would have prevented thousands of premature deaths._** If Congress is going to intervene, they need to see strong public pressure from people who refuse to stay quiet as Trump rolls back life-saving climate protections. **[Take a minute to read our full message below, then send your letters to demand Congress stop Trump’s fossil fuel giveaway >>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121612824/590019282/-737942839?source=email&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjane-bax-email-ja%3Frefcode%3Dea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6%26ms%3Dea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&refcode=ea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ms=ea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzY2Y4MDJlLTAyZGQtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMGQzYTFlYzhjNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=EJSFsKyLBEujHn1iI4bcvqKQ3xAn_xmyo65q_o6Lv7w=&emci=bdee29cf-fcdc-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4823105)** | [**CONDEMN TRUMP’S FOSSIL FUEL GIVEAWAY →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121612825/590019283/-737942839?source=email&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fjane-bax-email-ja%3Frefcode%3Dea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6%26ms%3Dea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&refcode=ea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ms=ea.23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjIzY2Y4MDJlLTAyZGQtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMGQzYTFlYzhjNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=EJSFsKyLBEujHn1iI4bcvqKQ3xAn_xmyo65q_o6Lv7w=&emci=bdee29cf-fcdc-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=23cf802e-02dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4823105)

Let’s do this.
Team JanePAC
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
**_The Washington Post: “EPA to abandon air pollution rule that would prevent thousands of U.S. deaths”_**
**[Scientists warn this decision will mean dirtier air, more asthma attacks, and thousands of preventable deaths. Trump’s EPA is _knowingly_ putting our lives at risk. We’ve come to expect the worst from the Trump administration. But even for them, undoing a rule designed solely to _save human lives_ crosses a terrifying line. Here’s what you need to know:
**_The situation:_** Trump’s EPA is gutting a Biden-era protection that limits fine-particle pollution — microscopic soot that comes from oil refineries, power plants, and vehicles. **This pollution invades the bloodstream and leads to heart attacks, strokes, lung cancer, and asthma.**
**_Why it matters:_** The Biden administration strengthened these protections to finally address the pollution burden placed on vulnerable communities who have been breathing dirty air for generations. The benefits were undeniable, and the science was clear.
**_What the data shows:_**_The EPA's own analysis showed this rule would prevent**4,500 premature deaths by 2032.**_
But fossil fuel companies and Republican attorneys general fought to stop it. And Trump’s EPA just sided with them by refusing to defend these life-saving protections in court.
Think about what this means for the children walking to school, breathing toxic air. Seniors whose lungs can’t withstand another spike in pollution. Communities living next to refineries and highways are forced to inhale soot day after day.
This isn’t just another policy rollback. For some of these frontline communities, it’s life or death.
**And it’s exactly why JanePAC fights to elect climate champions who will stand up to Donald Trump’s toxic agenda — and why your voice matters in these crucial moments.**

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
| | |
---

A fully loaded truck, flying down the interstate... no hands on the wheel, no one “in control” the way we’re used to.

No panic. No hesitation. Just a machine doing what humans used to do.

And [what happened next](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=66d61cc941b044d2a7d7d12012a26bc9&_e=h51PEWRHATqXFCnDVMdIq1u_hd8TLQENL2hTMA3aYF-OkPgSB4pqXTmMzVL3SY6BH4P6K8W33KxpzsZBsKRf45MfFYuamAZNIypAgG9itpXOx_-Sz6R-VyGOW3J80lskn3SkhfsV5a5GDr_5jboAOxT4578mD7h92QfttmvWnF9jNY4WontNVCcze6iCQbt05DBfFpXRBVFHx6-IJqUO68lRte3jA_PrLV3hBZzAuKzwxPDFxUWHryKZLpwc2DdO05ufeF1n5QctZX5UKVXhOvcVer3AmxluU0pYLv5ecB9ZtiAA0UJA4zV4QR9St5hV1TLz3nwsssjBZh7rp5vDN-lKuY8UHt8CL-_KJN9WKCB0X2wdt2lOZXNWTNSBCIIhhQ8agrM3L0ljaVREmWdJ0q9GTlAhFgXMjJr2zq15LwbaWPMorq7bIzMTVGl6qD4HhTTbm1V67x0CWIISQ8rbwdf7kmTABrCJPXiLspzXmyOAh2DKPyGsGWB_kcIc80mI9tCzlmrTGz2DCgpph6ohplCR7bCWy5DcsKkRXUSzkQ-dHnmp36pH881OXSEuhhCK4xGcnF2QKAClHhx3qcDeHw%3D%3D) changed my view of what “driving” means, what transport looks like, and how far tech has already gone.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

name: Team Goldman
subject: Dan is standing up to defend New Yorkers from ICE

body:
|
#
---
Peter, Donald Trump’s ICE secret agents are terrorizing our communities and ripping families apart. Dan Goldman is fighting back. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121613169/590020180/403085476?refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251219_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhM2MwNGM3NS0wM2RkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bOhZIYdF9JoA4lCmXEROzi0tc78lBv5okzxF6cnIPow=&emci=c806fc5b-ffdc-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=a3c04c75-03dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=13556823) The Trump Administration has mounted an all-out attack on the rule of law and due process, weaponizing the immigration system as a political tool and violating our fundamental rights in the process. **As an in-depth series in TPM documents in detail, Dan’s office is acting in real time to support immigrant families who are targeted by this administration.** Working with court watchers, advocacy groups and local clergy, our office is the hub of a rapid response triage center, where families come immediately after a loved one is arrested by ICE to receive help with food, shelter, health care and legal assistance. This is one of many ways Dan is standing up to defend New Yorkers from ICE. He’s demanding answers from the Department of Homeland Security. And he has introduced legislation like the No Secret Police Act, which would ban ICE agents from wearing face masks during arrests. Dan will never stop fighting back against Donald Trump’s inhumane and anti-American deportation agenda. Thank you for being a part of this fight, Team Goldman P.S. name: Derek Tran
subject: Me vs. the GOP machine
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121613934/590022856/-1516755731?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmY1MDg3OTMtMDRkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Ylkqvb0mE1WMVzdVro4xVCo4oB5zxMhPYwAQoghWVkk=)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121613938/590022866/-1516755731?refcode=em-12.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmY1MDg3OTMtMDRkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Ylkqvb0mE1WMVzdVro4xVCo4oB5zxMhPYwAQoghWVkk=)

I’m going to make a bold statement.

I just might have one of the toughest jobs in Congress:

\- I’m a first-time Congressman in the minority party of a deeply divided House.
\- I won CA-45 by a razor-thin margin of 653 votes. One of the only flips in 2024.

**Many, if not most, folks in my position would ride out the storm without making any waves. That’s not who I am.**

I’m the proud son of Vietnamese immigrants who gave up everything to escape to America after the Fall of Saigon. I dedicated my life to this country and my community -- serving in the Army, and now representing Southern California in Washington.

**I have a duty to serve with dignity and without fear. That means standing up for what’s right -- and oftentimes going toe-to-toe with the MAGA agenda that is leaving working families behind.**

Now, we’re about to turn the page into the midterm election year. I’m facing four GOP challengers in my first re-election campaign, and I won’t lie: the challenge ahead is big.

**[I’m standing by my values -- not taking donations from Big Pharma or Big Oil -- because it’s the right thing to do. If you’re with me, $3 would go far ahead of our final FEC deadline of 2025.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121613942/590022892/-1516755731?refcode=em-12.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmY1MDg3OTMtMDRkZC1mMDExLTgxOTQtMDAwZDNhMWVjOGM2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Ylkqvb0mE1WMVzdVro4xVCo4oB5zxMhPYwAQoghWVkk=)**

clean_body: | = = I’m going to make a bold statement. I just might have one of the toughest jobs in Congress: \- I’m a first-time Congressman in the minority party of a deeply divided House. \- I won CA-45 by a razor-thin margin of 653 votes. One of the only flips in 2024. Many, if not most, folks in my position would ride out the storm without making any waves. That’s not who I am. I’m the proud son of Vietnamese immigrants who gave up everything to escape to America after the Fall of Saigon. I dedicated my life to this country and my community -- serving in the Army, and now representing Southern California in Washington. I have a duty to serve with dignity and without fear. That means standing up for what’s right -- and oftentimes going toe-to-toe with the MAGA agenda that is leaving working families behind. Now, we’re about to turn the page into the midterm election year. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Experimental military tech. body:
PRESS RELEASE: REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN FILES LEGISLATION NECESSARY TO COMPLETE HIS FREEDOM 1-2-3 PROPERTY TAX REPLACEMENT PLAN

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/repryanchamberlin/press-release-rep-ryan-chamberlin-files-legislation-necessary-to-complete-his-freedom-1-2-3-property-tax-replacement-plan?e=d1c1b9864f)
---
|
---
| | | | | |
# REP. RYAN CHAMBERLIN FILES LEGISLATION NECESSARY TO COMPLETE HIS FREEDOM 1-2-3 PROPERTY TAX REPLACEMENT PLAN
---
| |
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
December 19, 2025

**Contact:** Rep. Ryan Chamberlin
**Email:** ryan@ryanchamberlin.com

**Belleview, Fla.** — Rep. Ryan Chamberlin has filed House Joint Resolution 787, House Bill 789, and House Bill 791. These three pieces of legislation, if passed, would implement his full replacement plan for property taxes. Chamberlin's legislation, in conjunction with an affirmative vote for a Constitutional amendment repealing property taxes, would completely replace property taxes with a sales and use tax and several consumption-based surtaxes.

“Speaker Danny Perez and House Property Tax Committee Chairman Toby Overdorf have advanced several excellent policy proposals for addressing Florida’s property tax crisis,” said Rep. Chamberlin. “They are providing options that would create relief while balancing the priorities of our other branches of government. But I wanted to file the remainder of my bills to chart a future discussion on fully replacing property tax revenues with other sources of revenue.”

In September of this year, Rep. Chamberlin outlined his conceptual plan to eliminate property taxes and make Florida the first state in the nation with no income tax and no property tax. However, Rep. Chamberlin acknowledges the impossibility of eliminating property taxes without providing for another revenue source to pay for roads, law enforcement, and schools. His plan focuses on transaction fees and consumption taxes as a means of placing a significant portion of the tax burden on tourists and visitors.

Chamberlin added: “Until we stop government from owning all property and forcing we the people to rent for the rest of our lives, we will never be free. I want to make permanent home and business ownership part of the free state of Florida story.”

Chamberlin already filed Freedom 1 which rolls back property taxes to the more affordable 2022-2023 tax revenue collections. Now his Freedom 2 bill eliminates all property taxes while Freedom 3 provides a replacement revenue source.

Chamberlin's legislation, in conjunction with an affirmative vote for a Constitutional amendment repealing property taxes, would completely replace property taxes with a sales and use tax and several consumption-based surtaxes. “Speaker Danny Perez and House Property Tax Committee Chairman Toby Overdorf have advanced several excellent policy proposals for addressing Florida’s property tax crisis,” said Rep. Chamberlin. “They are providing options that would create relief while balancing the priorities of our other branches of government. But I wanted to file the remainder of my bills to chart a future discussion on fully replacing property tax revenues with other sources of revenue.” In September of this year, Rep. Chamberlin outlined his conceptual plan to eliminate property taxes and make Florida the first state in the nation with no income tax and no property tax. However, Rep. Chamberlin acknowledges the impossibility of eliminating property taxes without providing for another revenue source to pay for roads, law enforcement, and schools. His plan focuses on transaction fees and consumption taxes as a means of placing a significant portion of the tax burden on tourists and visitors. Chamberlin added: “Until we stop government from owning all property and forcing we the people to rent for the rest of our lives, we will never be free. I want to make permanent home and business ownership part of the free state of Florida story.” Chamberlin already filed Freedom 1 which rolls back property taxes to the more affordable 2022-2023 tax revenue collections. Now his Freedom 2 bill eliminates all property taxes while Freedom 3 provides a replacement revenue source. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2025 Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative, All rights reserved. Paid by Ryan Chamberlin, Republican, for State Representative Our mailing address is: Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative526 E Park AveTallahassee, FL 32301-2568 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or |

# Bitcoin hoarders face index exile shock
MicroStrategy and other bitcoin-buying firms **[risk exclusion from major stock indexes](https://substack.com/redirect/aa7d70c6-1a14-4cc4-b8b9-377118979f4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/aa7d70c6-1a14-4cc4-b8b9-377118979f4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)like MSCI over their reclassification as Digital Asset Treasury DAT companies, potentially triggering up to $9 billion in share outflows and chilling the crypto treasury sector amid ongoing consultations.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ea880290-f08c-48a2-a041-04e5a020bcb3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Shares Skyrocketed Then Crashed** : MicroStrategy's stock surged 3,000% after starting bitcoin purchases in 2020 but has fallen 43% this year amid cryptocurrency's decline.
* **Outflows Could Hit Billions** : Analysts estimate $2.8 billion in forced sales from MSCI exclusion alone, rising to $8.8 billion if other indexes like Nasdaq 100 were to follow suit.
* **Leaders Decry Discrimination** : MicroStrategy's Saylor argue the proposal unfairly targets crypto firms, shutting them out of the $15 trillion passive-investment market.
* **Broader Indexes May Align** : Experts predict most equity indexes will adopt similar exclusions, amplifying challenges for firms funding token buys through stock and debt.

* * *
* * *
**[Together with Ifrah Law](https://substack.com/redirect/f510740c-8dc7-41d3-bf3a-86e8ea45f09f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

Led by a managing partner who has been **[at the center of every significant court case affecting online gaming,](https://substack.com/redirect/f510740c-8dc7-41d3-bf3a-86e8ea45f09f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Ifrah Law operates at the cutting edge of technology, innovation, and regulation.
Its lawyers represent industry players throughout the entire business cycle, from the formation of a corporation or licensing relationship, through marketing, partnering, growth, and disputes, to profitable exits.
We're proud to share that all partners at Ifrah Law have been ranked by Chambers and Partners, the gold standard for evaluating legal excellence worldwide.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f510740c-8dc7-41d3-bf3a-86e8ea45f09f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

# AI set to outperform human advisors in the realm or wealth management
Artificial intelligence is **[poised to revolutionize wealth management,](https://substack.com/redirect/a395728e-c3b9-423f-a2aa-11db87384196?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** outperforming most human financial advisors by delivering emotion-free, data-driven guidance and 24/7 real-time monitoring that could democratize high-quality advice for everyday investors.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a395728e-c3b9-423f-a2aa-11db87384196?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**Discipline Beats Emotional Pitfalls** : AI sidesteps greed and fear, sticking to probabilities and rules for consistent decisions that human advisors often compromise under client or media pressure.
**Continuous Oversight Enhances Outcomes** : Unlike sporadic human meetings, AI tracks spending patterns, debt, investments, risks, and taxes instantly, providing immediate feedback to optimize financial health.
**Low-Cost Subscriptions Transform Access** : For Netflix-like fees, AI offers commission-free coaching, stress-testing decisions, and calendar-integrated plans, exposing mediocre advisory services.
**Top Advisors Embrace Hybrid Model** : Elite humans will use AI for calculations and execution, focusing on intuition, family dynamics, and preventing life-altering mistakes in a modernized industry.
* * *
* * *
# Google cloud seals epic $10B security deal with long time partner
In a major boost for Alphabet's cloud arm, Google Cloud announced its **[largest security services partnership](https://substack.com/redirect/05dbeb58-5a55-4de9-8a3a-87d9af61cf7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with cybersecurity leader Palo Alto Networks, committing nearly $10 billion over years to migrate platforms and pioneer AI-driven defenses against evolving cyber threats.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/05dbeb58-5a55-4de9-8a3a-87d9af61cf7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**AI Sparks Security Surge:** Executives note artificial intelligence has massively increased demand for advanced cybersecurity, akin to early cloud-era threats.
**Attackers Exploit Generative Tools:** Cybercriminals increasingly use AI for attacks, mirroring defenses' adoption of the technology.
**Heavy Investments in Sector:** Google eyes $32 billion Wiz acquisition; Palo Alto launches AI offerings and plans $3.35 billion Chronosphere buy.
**Deep-Rooted Partnership History:** Google and PAlo Alto have been collaborating since 2018, bolstered by Palo Alto CEO's prior long tenure at Google.
* * *
* * *
# Hard assets smash records on future rate cut bets
As US inflation slows to its slowest pace since early 2021, gold and silver prices are **[surging near all-time highs,](https://substack.com/redirect/aa7d70c6-1a14-4cc4-b8b9-377118979f4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** driven by bets on further Federal Reserve rate cuts amid rising geopolitical tensions.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/41bd47b5-0699-411c-b9cf-1d91d1b6c80a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Gold Tops $4,330 Ounce** : Spot gold hits $4,330 per ounce, marking a two-thirds rise this year, as falling interest rates boost non-yielding assets.
* **Metals Surge in Value** : Silver has more than doubled in 2025, with platinum surging above $1,980 per ounce--its highest since 2008.
* **Central Banks Drive Demand** : Elevated central-bank purchases and ETF inflows underpin the rally, delivering the best annual performances since 1979.
* **Goldman Forecasts Price Climb** : Analysts predict structurally high demand and Fed support will push gold higher, despite ambiguous central bank signals.

## [These "Hard Asset Superheroes" reign in the age of debt and uncertainty ](https://substack.com/redirect/cd088574-c08e-4a0f-bd22-c53951c91cff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [The Capitalist](https://substack.com/profile/333697867-the-capitalist)| | *
---|---|---
| Oct 11
[](https://substack.com/redirect/cd088574-c08e-4a0f-bd22-c53951c91cff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[The world is drowning in debt and investors are seeking refuge in assets that promise stability amid economic turmoil. The so-called "debasement trade"--a strategic pivot toward hard assets like precious metals - and more recently Bitcoin - as hedges against fiat currency erosion--has gained unprecedented momentum.](https://substack.com/redirect/cd088574-c08e-4a0f-bd22-c53951c91cff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* * *
* * *
# Data center deals shatter records amid AI jitters
Global data center deals **[hit a record $61 billion in 2025,](https://substack.com/redirect/b9a7179d-50b0-4bd6-a6e2-46b447da370c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** driven by AI infrastructure demands, even as investors fret over soaring valuations and hyperscalers' reliance on massive debt financing.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5dc2c627-fcd1-4933-9077-a321f330e430?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Debt Issuance Surges** : Hyperscalers doubled debt to $182 billion this year, with Meta raising nearly half its $62 billion since 2022 in 2025 alone.
* **U.S. Dominates Market** : Most transactions occurred in America, far outpacing Europe's slower growth, while the Middle East emerges as a new AI powerhouse.
* **Transactions Exceed Last Year** : Over 100 deals in the first 11 months surpassed all of 2024, fueled by a global construction frenzy for energy-intensive workloads.
* **Future Valuations Rise** : Analysts foresee strong AI demand in 2026, potentially inflating valuations further amid energy shortages and robust M&A activity.

Bitcoin holding firms like Michael Saylor's Microstrategy face an uncertain future | | | Dec 19 | | | | | | | | | | | | Hello Capitalists, Here is everything you should be following today: Bitcoin holding firms face an uncertain future in 2026 AI is about to wade in to the wealth management industry Google inks billion dollar AI security deal Gold and Silver eye future rate cuts to fuel their rise Data Center investment deals in 2025 broke $61 Billion dollars Today 's markets + assets: ✅ DOW: 48273.94 (⬆️ 0.67%) ✅ S&P: 6838.57 (⬆️ 0.94%) ✅ NASDAQ: 23283.41 (⬆️ 1.20%) ⚠ ️🔴CBOE VIX Volatility Index: 15.26 (⬇️ 9.54%) ✅ Gold: $4,387.00 (⬆️ 0.33%) ✅ Silver: $67.65 (⬆️ 2.60%) ✅ Bitcoin: $87,273 (⬆️ 0.25%) The Capitalist is a reader-supported publication Reject Corporate Left-Wing Journalism Bitcoin hoarders face index exile shock MicroStrategy and other bitcoin-buying firms MSCI over their reclassification as Digital Asset Treasury DAT companies, potentially triggering up to $9 billion in share outflows and chilling the crypto treasury sector amid ongoing consultations. | | ---|---|--- Shares Skyrocketed Then Crashed : MicroStrategy's stock surged 3,000% after starting bitcoin purchases in 2020 but has fallen 43% this year amid cryptocurrency's decline. Outflows Could Hit Billions : Analysts estimate $2.8 billion in forced sales from MSCI exclusion alone, rising to $8.8 billion if other indexes like Nasdaq 100 were to follow suit. Leaders Decry Discrimination : MicroStrategy's Saylor argue the proposal unfairly targets crypto firms, shutting them out of the $15 trillion passive-investment market. Broader Indexes May Align : Experts predict most equity indexes will adopt similar exclusions, amplifying challenges for firms funding token buys through stock and debt. Led by a managing partner who has been Ifrah Law operates at the cutting edge of technology, innovation, and regulation. Its lawyers represent industry players throughout the entire business cycle, from the formation of a corporation or licensing relationship, through marketing, partnering, growth, and disputes, to profitable exits. We're proud to share that all partners at Ifrah Law have been ranked by Chambers and Partners, the gold standard for evaluating legal excellence worldwide. | | ---|---|--- AI set to outperform human advisors in the realm or wealth management Artificial intelligence is outperforming most human financial advisors by delivering emotion-free, data-driven guidance and 24/7 real-time monitoring that could democratize high-quality advice for everyday investors. | | ---|---|--- Discipline Beats Emotional Pitfalls : AI sidesteps greed and fear, sticking to probabilities and rules for consistent decisions that human advisors often compromise under client or media pressure. Continuous Oversight Enhances Outcomes : Unlike sporadic human meetings, AI tracks spending patterns, debt, investments, risks, and taxes instantly, providing immediate feedback to optimize financial health. Low-Cost Subscriptions Transform Access : For Netflix-like fees, AI offers commission-free coaching, stress-testing decisions, and calendar-integrated plans, exposing mediocre advisory services. Top Advisors Embrace Hybrid Model : Elite humans will use AI for calculations and execution, focusing on intuition, family dynamics, and preventing life-altering mistakes in a modernized industry. Google cloud seals epic $10B security deal with long time partner In a major boost for Alphabet's cloud arm, Google Cloud announced its with cybersecurity leader Palo Alto Networks, committing nearly $10 billion over years to migrate platforms and pioneer AI-driven defenses against evolving cyber threats. | | ---|---|--- AI Sparks Security Surge: Executives note artificial intelligence has massively increased demand for advanced cybersecurity, akin to early cloud-era threats. Attackers Exploit Generative Tools: Cybercriminals increasingly use AI for attacks, mirroring defenses' adoption of the technology. Heavy Investments in Sector: Google eyes $32 billion Wiz acquisition; Palo Alto launches AI offerings and plans $3.35 billion Chronosphere buy. Deep-Rooted Partnership History: Google and PAlo Alto have been collaborating since 2018, bolstered by Palo Alto CEO's prior long tenure at Google. Hard assets smash records on future rate cut bets As US inflation slows to its slowest pace since early 2021, gold and silver prices are driven by bets on further Federal Reserve rate cuts amid rising geopolitical tensions. | | ---|---|--- Gold Tops $4,330 Ounce : Spot gold hits $4,330 per ounce, marking a two-thirds rise this year, as falling interest rates boost non-yielding assets. Metals Surge in Value : Silver has more than doubled in 2025, with platinum surging above $1,980 per ounce--its highest since 2008. Central Banks Drive Demand : Elevated central-bank purchases and ETF inflows underpin the rally, delivering the best annual performances since 1979. Goldman Forecasts Price Climb : Analysts predict structurally high demand and Fed support will push gold higher, despite ambiguous central bank signals. | | | | ---|---|--- | Oct 11 | Data center deals shatter records amid AI jitters Global data center deals driven by AI infrastructure demands, even as investors fret over soaring valuations and hyperscalers' reliance on massive debt financing. | | ---|---|--- Debt Issuance Surges : Hyperscalers doubled debt to $182 billion this year, with Meta raising nearly half its $62 billion since 2022 in 2025 alone. U.S. Dominates Market : Most transactions occurred in America, far outpacing Europe's slower growth, while the Middle East emerges as a new AI powerhouse. Transactions Exceed Last Year : Over 100 deals in the first 11 months surpassed all of 2024, fueled by a global construction frenzy for energy-intensive workloads. Future Valuations Rise : Analysts foresee strong AI demand in 2026, potentially inflating valuations further amid energy shortages and robust M&A activity. Folks,
As Jocelyn criss-crosses the state to talk to voters, she’s been hearing the same thing: already sky-high costs _keep on going up_ , and Michiganders are worried about how they’ll make ends meet.
**Why? Because of Donald Trump's reckless tariffs, which John James has stood behind.** [DONATE TO ELECT JOCELYN BENSON](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hVxyII&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251219_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=1157%2E181023%2EP4o_jN)

name: NPR + PBS Alert via Our Revolution
subject: The end of PBS?!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ]Our Revolution Logo

Peter Willis,

Public media is hanging on by a thread after Trump's $1.1 BILLION in
funding cuts to NPR and PBS earlier this year. And the dam is breaking:
Arkansas’ statewide public television network just announced it will end
its affiliation with PBS thanks to funding cuts, saying it cannot afford
PBS membership fees.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34896?source=email-20251219&t=1011&akid=9992%2E5885482%2EyLPxPK ]Headline: Arkansas will become first state to end PBS affiliation

Meanwhile, Trump’s busy rubber stamping mega-mergers for his oligarch
media buddies – tightening their grip on what we see, hear, and read.

That’s why Our Revolution is fighting back. Right now, the Senate is
working on its 2026 budget and can RESTORE funding for public media before
it’s too late. But it’s going to take a MASSIVE public outcry to force
them to do it.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34896?source=email-20251219&t=1012&akid=9992%2E5885482%2EyLPxPK ]We CANNOT let Trump and his oligarchs steal our media. Will you
urgently add your name now to demand Congress restore funding for PBS and
NPR NOW, before it’s too late?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34896?source=email-20251219&t=1013&akid=9992%2E5885482%2EyLPxPK ]ADD YOUR NAME

As Trump looks to hand full control of the media to his oligarch friends,
he slashed funding for NPR and PBS – forcing layoffs, killing local shows,
and risking total shutdowns where stations can’t survive without federal
help. Arkansas is just the beginning. PBS has already announced a
devastating 15% staff cut and a budget cut of 21%.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34896?source=email-20251219&t=1014&akid=9992%2E5885482%2EyLPxPK ]Headline: PBS slashes 15% of its staff after federal funding cuts

In some rural areas, the local PBS channel is the ONLY place families can
get tornado warnings, school closures, or emergency weather updates. For
over 50 years, PBS has brought trusted learning into our homes. It’s given
us Big Bird and Frontline. It served as the only source of local news and
safety alerts for millions – especially in rural and working-class
communities.

It’s not too late to reverse course, but Congress is racing to finish the
budget by January. Which gives us just weeks to flood Congress with our
demands. Our Revolution is ready to deliver our voices straight to
Congress in these crucial moments, but first we need to come together in
massive numbers to show that our public outrage cannot be ignored.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34896?source=email-20251219&t=1015&akid=9992%2E5885482%2EyLPxPK ]We CANNOT let Trump and his oligarchs get away with silencing our
public media for their own gain. ACT NOW and tell Congress to restore
public media funding in the 2026 budget before more communities are left
in the dark.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34896?source=email-20251219&t=1016&akid=9992%2E5885482%2EyLPxPK ]ADD YOUR NAME

When we organize, we win.

Our Revolution

name: Powered by People
subject: We’re finishing up our 2026 budget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240550%2E444211%2ENEa96a)
As we look toward 2026, the stakes for Texas have never been higher.
At Powered by People, we know that victory isn't won in the final weeks of an election. It’s won right now, in the quiet months, by building the grassroots infrastructure that can withstand anything the opposition throws our way.
Our 2026 budget plans are the most ambitious yet, and to pull this off, we need to secure our resources early. [**Can you chip in $3 today to help build our organizing and voter registration program ahead of time?**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251219.rBwte_&refcode=ema20251219-240550&t=3&akid=240550%2E444211%2ENEa96a)
Here is how we plan to put your contribution to work:
* **Expanding our reach:** We’re scaling up our proven 2024 model, moving from 30,000 deep-engagement voters to over 100,000 voters by November 3, 2026.
* **Winning across the ballot:** We’re focused on every level of the ballot. That means flipping key State House and Congressional districts, defeating Greg Abbott, and winning that U.S. Senate seat.
* **The college campus program:** We’re heading to campuses across Texas to organize the youngest, most diverse generation of voters in our history.
* **Building year-round power:** We don’t disappear after Election Day. We’re investing in grassroots organizing and community building to last beyond 2026.
* **The on-the-ground essentials:** Your support funds the hard work of registering voters, signing up volunteers, hosting town halls, and—most importantly—staying in touch with newly registered voters so they have everything they need to make their voices heard.

The only way to fight for our future and win key races is through the kind of large-scale, face-to-face organizing that Powered by People does best.
[**Will you chip in $3 to help ensure we have the resources to run our program as effectively as possible? When supporters like you step up together, we’re unstoppable.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251219.rBwte_&refcode=ema20251219-240550&t=4&akid=240550%2E444211%2ENEa96a)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251219.rBwte_&refcode=ema20251219-240550&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=240550%2E444211%2ENEa96a)**
Thank you,
Powered by People

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20251219-240550-footer&t=24&refcode2=240550_444211_NEa96a&akid=240550%2E444211%2ENEa96a)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/update-contact-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/)

Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople.org](http://www.poweredxpeople.org?akid=240550%2E444211%2ENEa96a), not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

subject: Do you know what happened on this day nearly 250 years ago?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
Many consider Valley Forge to be the birthplace of the American Army.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121616939/590033275/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNDgyZDVkYi0wYWRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DeiQ7zESJ6uN6HzPK9gtIbBYYQYMg0ij6YE66lo-lLI=&emci=d76bda1f-54dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3482d5db-0add-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=21278485)

Derek, did you know that on this day, December 19, almost 250 years ago George Washington's army arrived at Valley Forge during the Revolutionary War – right here in Pennsylvania's 6th District?

Many consider Valley Forge to be the **birthplace of the American Army** , with concepts of basic training and distinctive branches originating here. Today, we know this area as Valley Forge National Historical Park, which consists of 3,500 acres of green spaces, historic structures, and monuments.

**But as you read this, national parks like Valley Forge are at risk. **
**Trump and his GOP allies in Congress have recklessly and dangerously GUTTED FUNDING for national parks, forests, and our public lands. Public parks like Valley Forge are a national treasure, Derek – and it's up to us to protect them. **
**_[Please, will you sign Chrissy's petition in support of protecting our national parks today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121616941/590033281/-481502325?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1200-su-nd&refcode=jtk1200-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNDgyZDVkYi0wYWRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DeiQ7zESJ6uN6HzPK9gtIbBYYQYMg0ij6YE66lo-lLI=&emci=d76bda1f-54dc-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3482d5db-0add-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=21278485)_**

| Please don't scroll past this message.
We'll be direct: [Shawn Harris is running to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene and finally bring real leadership to GA-14 -- and this is our first, best chance in years to flip this district. Can you rush a donation to his campaign right now to help us take advantage of this incredible opportunity?](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NuBAxdRnBAv4xvAQuhSIDrtj7lN6I9OO4y6UiWPVaoodZHXUmucDlCo-xrplarIDzU278abR_frxhxS6ZPSt4ginpsf7y4EPiTiIvU_OgxyWQVs2KugGrR-rwTPgRhRvP5R3rSFVN05avnCroErWKiBULyY5-gvWP-KQvklXj0Vv01iWj0FWXNPG8Jos9AYGlRIAVKEJJO4Kvgj0Q6L0CFOyQ3s3mo9t&c=_kOsISMWCDv5QQY3rqnogTvg7gOQVTu9z-R4FkgZ7WGOLEiWPkTT2w==&ch=blts_Wwyhp2PcLUQiXtLmHVBMVFZql4uL1xxJbwCPWvenhKyW7GEXQ==)
With MTG resigning, the political landscape in Northwest Georgia has shifted for the first time in a decade. But while this is a major opportunity, it is not guaranteed.
Republicans will spend whatever it takes to keep this seat in the hands of another extremist.
And here's the truth: If we don't build early, we lose the chance to shape what GA-14 becomes next. We can't afford to wait until 2026 to get serious.
[If everyone reading this email chipped in just $10, Shawn's campaign would have the resources to launch the strongest, most competitive organizing effort this district has ever seen.](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NuBAxdRnBAv4xvAQuhSIDrtj7lN6I9OO4y6UiWPVaoodZHXUmucDlCo-xrplarIDzU278abR_frxhxS6ZPSt4ginpsf7y4EPiTiIvU_OgxyWQVs2KugGrR-rwTPgRhRvP5R3rSFVN05avnCroErWKiBULyY5-gvWP-KQvklXj0Vv01iWj0FWXNPG8Jos9AYGlRIAVKEJJO6Wk-JRXT5pPkEt6JRDXxn8&c=_kOsISMWCDv5QQY3rqnogTvg7gOQVTu9z-R4FkgZ7WGOLEiWPkTT2w==&ch=blts_Wwyhp2PcLUQiXtLmHVBMVFZql4uL1xxJbwCPWvenhKyW7GEXQ==)
But the reality is, most people won't donate. 99% of people reading this email won't give.
We are counting on the 1% who believe GA-14 deserves better -- who believe that we can replace chaos with competence, division with dignity, and headlines about feuds with headlines about progress.
[If electing a leader focused on rebuilding our district -- not tearing it apart -- is worth $10 to you, please: Chip in to support Shawn Harris and help us seize this once-in-a-generation chance to change GA-14.](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NuBAxdRnBAv4xvAQuhSIDrtj7lN6I9OO4y6UiWPVaoodZHXUmucDlCo-xrplarIDzU278abR_frxhxS6ZPSt4ginpsf7y4EPiTiIvU_OgxyWQVs2KugGrR-rwTPgRhRvP5R3rSFVN05avnCroErWKiBULyY5-gvWP-KQvklXj0Vv01iWj0FWXNPG8Jos9AYGlRIAVKEJJO7ba0JRxbzGzwD_Lp_tVVK-&c=_kOsISMWCDv5QQY3rqnogTvg7gOQVTu9z-R4FkgZ7WGOLEiWPkTT2w==&ch=blts_Wwyhp2PcLUQiXtLmHVBMVFZql4uL1xxJbwCPWvenhKyW7GEXQ==)

name: Ilhan Omar for Congress
subject: 🚨 Trump is investigating Ilhan.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
This is a major escalation. Help us fight back.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=2007%2E791427%2E1T3yZT)
[](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251219-1-300&t=2&akid=2007%2E791427%2E1T3yZT)
**Friend — This is a major escalation.**
Trump has been going after Ilhan for weeks, calling her “garbage”, insulting her religion, and spreading dangerous and racist conspiracy theories.
But now, Trump’s “Border Czar”, Tom Homan, has confirmed that **the Trump administration is**investigating Ilhan Omar** in an attempt to punish her with criminal charges.**
**[PLEASE, rush a donation to stand with Ilhan Omar and help us fight back against this sham investigation >>**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251219-1-300&t=3&akid=2007%2E791427%2E1T3yZT)
**[RUSH A DONATION](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251219-1-300&t=4&akid=2007%2E791427%2E1T3yZT)**
Friend — Make no mistake, this is a highly political investigation with no basis in reality.
This administration is getting their information from conspiracy theorists and amplifying long-debunked lies about Ilhan and her family.
But Trump’s goal isn’t to seek the truth. His goal is to use the power of the federal government to intimidate Ilhan into silence and scapegoat immigrants like her, just like what he is doing with his inhumane ICE raids and racial-profiling on the streets of Minneapolis.
Well, we’re not going to sit idly by while Trump makes a mockery of justice.
It’s our job to show the MAGA far-right that Ilhan has a huge grassroots movement behind her, and that if they want to come for her, they have to come for us, too.
**[Help us fight back against this sham investigation by chipping in to power our movement today. We need the help of everyone on this team to weather this storm and keep up the fight against Trump’s corrupt administration.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-dec25.HnwiDc&refcode=em251219-1-300&t=5&akid=2007%2E791427%2E1T3yZT)

We hope you will stand with Ilhan today.

Ilhan Omar for Congress
