clean_body: Something unusual just happened on Wall Street — and almost nobody’s paying attention. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Something unusual just happened on Wall Street — and almost nobody’s paying attention. name: Massachusetts Democratic Party
subject: Trump Truth: The Affordable Care Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Hello Peter,
Massachusetts ranks first in the nation in health care coverage, including system performance, access, affordability, and quality. We also have the lowest uninsured rate in the country – 2.1% [https://wbjournal.com/article/chia-mass-uninsured-rate-is-far-lower-than-nations-average/] – according to the Center for Health Information and Analysis.
Greater access to healthcare produces better health outcomes.
Governor Maura Healey has announced that more than 10,000 people have already dropped their MassHealth Connector plans in anticipation of premium spikes if the Affordable Care Act is allowed to expire at the end of December.
According to Alex Sheff of Health Care for All [https://www.wgbh.org/news/health/2025-12-09/expiring-subsidies-could-increase-health-premiums-for-400-000-massachusetts-residents] , the biggest impact will be on about 26,000 residents making just over 400% of the federal poverty level, which is just over $60,000 a year for an individual. In addition, approximately 400,000 Massachusetts residents who currently receive some level of premium assistance through the ACA will see higher costs.
Congressman Stephen Lynch told WCVB that the change will be dramatic [https://www.facebook.com/wcvb5/posts/congressman-stephen-lynch-said-the-change-will-be-dramatic/1390967766392641/] . A couple currently paying $5,000 a year for health coverage could see that cost rise to more than $20,000 in 2026 if the ACA is not extended. Without subsidies, he predicts many people will simply opt out of coverage.
Meanwhile, Trump’s so-called “One Big Beautiful Bill” would leave millions of people without Medicaid coverage , placing additional strain on our health care system. Hospitals — especially rural hospitals — will be hit hardest, and many could be forced to close.
Asked about the potential loss of coverage, President Trump’s response was to suggest [https://wbjournal.com/article/chia-mass-uninsured-rate-is-far-lower-than-nations-average/] : “Take money from insurers, hand it to consumers and let them buy their healthcare.” He offered no explanation for how that would lower costs and told reporters, “Don’t make it sound so bad.”
In Congress, the Senate voted to reject extending ACA subsidies. The House passed a discharge petition to force a vote on ACA subsidies but that vote will not take place until January 6. Current subsidies expire on December 31.
Federal Cuts to SNAP Benefits
Under the One Big Beautiful Bill, at least 150,000 Massachusetts residents [https://www.bostonglobe.com/2025/12/07/metro/massachusetts-food-pantries-snap-cuts/] could see their SNAP benefits reduced or eliminated. Children, seniors, and people with disabilities are among those who rely on SNAP to put food on the table.
Governor Healey has said the Commonwealth will not be able to replace lost federal benefits .
The Trump Administration is also demanding detailed data from “blue” states on SNAP recipients and has threatened to withhold benefits from states [https://www.boston.com/news/politics/2025/12/02/wu-healey-respond-as-trump-threatens-to-withhold-snap-funds-from-blue-states/] that do not comply. Veterans and people experiencing homelessness would be required to work to receive benefits, and the age requirement has been raised from 55 to 64 .
Food banks cannot make up the shortfall. Neither can individual states.
Massachusetts Job Losses Are Rising
Massachusetts, which previously enjoyed an unemployment rate below the national average, is now experiencing a 4.8% unemployment rate , compared to 4.6% nationally , according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics.
The Professional, Scientific, and Business Services [https://www.bls.gov/regions/northeast/news-release/countyemploymentandwages_massachusetts.htm] sector experienced notable declines in early 2025. With federal cuts to research and development funding, Massachusetts stands to lose a major source of employment and income.
The Bottom Line
Health care coverage, food assistance, and jobs are under threat, and the people who will pay the price are Massachusetts families.
This is Trump’s affordability “hoax”!
https://www.facebook.com/massdems [https://www.facebook.com/massdems] https://twitter.com/massdems [https://twitter.com/massdems] https://instagram.com/massdems [https://instagram.com/massdems] https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems [https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems]
Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
Massachusetts Democratic Party
11 Beacon St
Suite 410
Boston, MA 02108
United States

body:

If you'd prefer, you can [unsubscribe here](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/K-_-4uI0EkWyg9pVsZKBKQ2?unsubscribedata=87AHInBfbrKEJbYv%2BQ5TgVvsgWNV1vcF3lncCpp1nTegWiLkl6IF%2B2VGUXsFmXbA2hsi6y3z4lL0kDOiR3v5u9PC8mhDoC77%2BLr2hU3hXiy67OSw1TFJdGBa6IwNoovyh%2BmFTdNgkoognwJRD4DkfjAnW%2B6n4of2daBh9IiFOUk16IACslT8Bs5Zg0uh80MAHePcXuaPdAWkez2xxXLc3qlO0bfq7%2B1mwsm5BmnyLLmhtNmn07Yblxnx8PA%2B7SiEY179grdfoZCTI7fAJrgC2ANlBcUsZFn8lrAmxQUlUabb1K9Ma32iolQfJjpKFohL%2F3rIYXTB4xi70S4FWY31%2FZJ1z%2FxwehXupJAR4KkkVVnn0IM8jHUTfX721Q%2BBBQ7Qp7QBYDow9vP4YIrXFj2fzn6P4ACB438a1meO7jAwo9AfJeyfBrCF%2Bi%2FVDCD9MYAT).
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121627861/590069234/1108982690?amount=25&amounts=250,100,50,25,10,5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.12.19_Laubacher-Humbly-Split_B2_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDY1MDI4Ni0yNmRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JOQMQNBcAyZkbY0yvURmbP_emgm5vQBqhBMPckInu_w=) Peter, It’s Eileen Laubacher, the retired U.S. Navy Admiral and Democrat running against Lauren Boebert here in Colorado. And I am emailing to humbly ask if you can make a donation to my campaign for Congress today to help me defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat blue. Lauren Boebert barely won reelection against Adam Frisch. He lost by _just 546 votes_ in what was the closest race in the entire country. **Now, after retiring as a two-star Admiral, I’m running to defeat Lauren Boebert because I know we can send her packing and turn this seat blue – but I’m counting on grassroots donors like you to give now if we’re going to do it.** [**_Please, will you split a donation to my campaign and Raja Krishnamoorthi's now to help me defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House and Senate for Democrats? Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.

name: James Carville
subject: A long freakin' year.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Y'all -- it's been a long freakin' year.

**But it ain't over yet, and we can't close out 2025 slacking off. We've gotta set the tone for 2026 and beyond -- and we do that by finishing strong.**

**[We've got one last goal to hit before the year is over -- we gotta show Trump and his lackeys in the Senate that we're ready to take back the majority. So I'm askin', will you donate $5 to help us flip the Senate and hit our $500,000 end-of-year goal? Your gift will even be tripled, but only if you act fast!>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/22327?amount=5&t=7&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251219-JamesCarville-CarvilleEOYFRRF-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251219-JamesCarville-CarvilleEOYFRRF-ETG-sl1&akid=22148%2E9716520%2EAShfC1)**
| | [DONATE $5 (BECOMES $15)](https://go.dscc.org/go/22327?amount=5&t=8&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251219-JamesCarville-CarvilleEOYFRRF-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251219-JamesCarville-CarvilleEOYFRRF-ETG-sl1&akid=22148%2E9716520%2EAShfC1)
---

Here's my New Year's resolution, y'all: **Keep fighting. Don't give an inch. Make 'em pay for their lies, half-hearted promises, and extremism by beating 'em out in every race possible.**

But staying in the trenches is easier said than done. Believe me, if I could do it alone, I wouldn't be buggin' ya right now! I need _your_ help, because we're at our strongest when we fight back together -- so here I am in your inbox before the end of the year.

When the DSCC reports their FINAL numbers to the FEC, I want 'em to say that Senate Democrats closed out 2025 stronger than the GOP did.

But we don't have much time left to hit that $500,000 goal and get the midterm fight off to a ROARING start. And just between you and me, we ain't there yet. I'm asking because I need you -- yes, _YOU_! So please, don't wait 'til the year ends:

**[It's now or never, so let's get it done. Rush $5 or more before the year's over to triple your gift, stick it to Trump's lackeys, and start 2026 ready to keep up the fight>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/22327?amount=5&t=9&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251219-JamesCarville-CarvilleEOYFRRF-ETG-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251219-JamesCarville-CarvilleEOYFRRF-ETG-sl1&akid=22148%2E9716520%2EAShfC1)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved your payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

clean_body: | | | Y'all -- it's been a long freakin' year. But it ain't over yet, and we can't close out 2025 slacking off. We've gotta set the tone for 2026 and beyond -- and we do that by finishing strong. | | Here's my New Year's resolution, y'all: Keep fighting. Don't give an inch. Make 'em pay for their lies, half-hearted promises, and extremism by beating 'em out in every race possible. But staying in the trenches is easier said than done. Believe me, if I could do it alone, I wouldn't be buggin' ya right now! I need your help, because we're at our strongest when we fight back together -- so here I am in your inbox before the end of the year. When the DSCC reports their FINAL numbers to the FEC, I want 'em to say that Senate Democrats closed out 2025 stronger than the GOP did. But we don't have much time left to hit that $500,000 goal and get the midterm fight off to a ROARING start. And just between you and me, we ain't there yet. I'm asking because I need you -- yes, YOU! So please, don't wait 'til the year ends: We need your help to fight for the Senate. subject: Christina and Janelle understand, while Republicans are out of touch
body:
A recent Fox News national poll shows 76% of voters view the economy negatively. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121628533/590071647/-1510725204?refcode=WUEFEL47_20251219_TrumpApproval_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1057506&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ODA1NjkxYy0yYWRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jtcCa0xGYPFKblcgfCF23zb2AkwwxpAXp6OYBgZTdfI=) Peter — The numbers are impossible to ignore. **A recent _Fox News_ national poll shows 76% of voters view the economy negatively.** Families are struggling to pay for groceries, utilities, health care, and housing — and nearly half of all Americans say these essentials are getting harder to afford. **They blame Donald Trump. Republicans — and Trump himself — are out of touch. While everyday Americans feel the squeeze, Trump gave himself an "A-plus-plus-plus-plus-plus" grade on the economy. The disconnect is staggering, and frustration is boiling over.

That's why our candidates have a real shot to break through. We're endorsing women who understand what families are going through:
* **Christina Hines (MI-10)** and **JoAnna Mendoza (AZ-06)** , moms balancing child care while running for office.
* **Lindsay James (IA-02)** , a pastor, and **Janelle Stelson (PA-10)** , a reporter, both spent their careers listening to their communities and speaking up for their needs.
* **Kaela Berg (MN-02)** and **Christina Bohannan (IA-01)** , both know the reality of navigating a health insurance system that is working against them.

They live the reality of affordability. They know the struggles of juggling bills, child care, health care, and work, and they're running to fix it. They're ready to challenge the Republicans who ignore us and fight for families.

clean_body: A recent Fox News national poll shows 76% of voters view the economy negatively. | = Peter — The numbers are impossible to ignore. A recent Fox News national poll shows 76% of voters view the economy negatively. Families are struggling to pay for groceries, utilities, health care, and housing — and nearly half of all Americans say these essentials are getting harder to afford. They blame Donald Trump. And they should. = | = Republicans — and Trump himself — are out of touch. While everyday Americans feel the squeeze, Trump gave himself an "A-plus-plus-plus-plus-plus" grade on the economy. The disconnect is staggering, and frustration is boiling over. That's why our candidates have a real shot to break through. We're endorsing women who understand what families are going through: Christina Hines (MI-10) and JoAnna Mendoza (AZ-06) , moms balancing child care while running for office. Lindsay James (IA-02) , a pastor, and Janelle Stelson (PA-10) , a reporter, both spent their careers listening to their communities and speaking up for their needs. Kaela Berg (MN-02) and Christina Bohannan (IA-01)** , both know the reality of navigating a health insurance system that is working against them. They live the reality of affordability. They know the struggles of juggling bills, child care, health care, and work, and they're running to fix it. They're ready to challenge the Republicans who ignore us and fight for families. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks for stepping up, Ben Ray (he/him/his) Deputy Chief Campaigns Officer, Federal & Gubernatorial, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Seth Moulton
subject: Release the files.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth for Massachusetts Checks may also be mailed to:
body:
Tonight.

| |
---

Friend,

Let me be clear.

Donald Trump’s name appears in the Epstein files. And the bipartisan law requiring the release of those unclassified records is clear.

The deadline is tonight.

Those records should be released.
Victims’ names should be redacted.
But every public official and public figure named must be disclosed, without regard to reputation or politics. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121628890/590072845/-662937607?refcode=251219_sm_files&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjI1ZWNiNi0yYWRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PJG7kG1uG2bjHMYikH3Sl2WV84f5J8Rdysn46_pzddI=&emci=ca39552a-13dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=7625ecb6-2add-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=194769) For years, too many people have turned a blind eye to the obvious connections between Trump and Epstein. Too many have acted as if wealth or political influence buys a permanent Get Out of Jail Free card.

I’m tired of it.

Leadership isn’t about staying quiet when the truth is inconvenient. It’s about saying what’s right and standing behind it when it counts. No administration gets to ignore the law. No president, sitting or former, gets a pass.

**No one is above the law.**

That’s what this campaign is about: courage, integrity, and the kind of straight talk that helps rebuild trust in government.

**[If you believe the law should be followed and the truth should come out, help power this campaign through the end of the year.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121628891/590072846/2090747700?refcode=251219_sm_files_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjI1ZWNiNi0yYWRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PJG7kG1uG2bjHMYikH3Sl2WV84f5J8Rdysn46_pzddI=)** | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121628892/590072847/1375639745?amp;refcode=251219_sm_files&refcode=251219_sm_files&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjI1ZWNiNi0yYWRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PJG7kG1uG2bjHMYikH3Sl2WV84f5J8Rdysn46_pzddI=)

name: Rev. Raphael Warnock
subject: What I told Chris Hayes on MS NOW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warnock for Georgia
body:
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121629331/590074224/-1698202107?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk5682-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTEvMS85MDkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZDVlZWY2MC0yY2RkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ruIi_FTMV_ocFI9VNhdU4AV8cusXqn2Os353veSU-D4=)
Friend, Donald Trump posted an unhinged rant about me on _Truth Social_ this week for speaking on my faith. I shared my response on _All In with Chris Hayes_ , but I also wanted to take a moment and share it with you directly.
Donald Trump has got a lot of nerve saying that I'm using faith to divide people. This is a man who literally had protesters gassed and beaten down so he could hold up a bible in front of a church. A man who is taking health care away from millions of Americans and creating fear and terror throughout immigrant communities.
**My faith is not a weapon. It's a bridge.** And I'd invite the president to come over to my church. Maybe we ought to have some bible study, because apparently he doesn't know the faith that I talk about on Sunday mornings.
**Until then, I will continue to speak out as loudly and as often as I can to fight back against the harm that Donald Trump is inflicting on families across the country.**
[**_If there’s anything you can spare right now to help me fight back against Trump’s attacks, I’d be so grateful for your support. Thanks for pitching in to support my campaign._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121629332/590074225/-1698202107?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&amount=5&refcode=jtk5682-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTEvMS85MDkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZDVlZWY2MC0yY2RkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ruIi_FTMV_ocFI9VNhdU4AV8cusXqn2Os353veSU-D4=)

clean_body: | | | = Friend, Donald Trump posted an unhinged rant about me on Truth Social this week for speaking on my faith. I shared my response on All In with Chris Hayes , but I also wanted to take a moment and share it with you directly. Donald Trump has got a lot of nerve saying that I'm using faith to divide people. This is a man who literally had protesters gassed and beaten down so he could hold up a bible in front of a church. A man who is taking health care away from millions of Americans and creating fear and terror throughout immigrant communities. My faith is not a weapon. It's a bridge. And I'd invite the president to come over to my church. Maybe we ought to have some bible study, because apparently he doesn't know the faith that I talk about on Sunday mornings. Until then, I will continue to speak out as loudly and as often as I can to fight back against the harm that Donald Trump is inflicting on families across the country. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thanks for all you do. name: Ro Khanna
subject: No One Is Above the Law – Not Even the Epstein Class
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
BREAKING: After a year of willful disregard for the law, the Department of Justice has begun releasing documents under my bipartisan Epstein Files Transparency Act, but it has not yet fully complied with the law.
Let me be clear about what this law demands.
It requires all unclassified Epstein records to be released by today, in a searchable and downloadable format.
It also requires DOJ to explain any redactions in the Federal Register and to provide Congress, within 15 days, a detailed explanation of anything they are withholding as classified.
That law is now binding.
Today’s release is a historic moment for survivors . DOJ has produced hundreds of thousands of pages – more than ten times what they previously gave Congress. If these documents are meaningful, not overly redacted, and paired with a clear plan for full disclosure, that is a positive step.
But it is not enough. Sign here to demand that the DOJ provide a timeline for the full release of the Epstein files. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/epstein-files-release-timeline-petition?contactdata=5jrYr1tKAPGt0MbbSzoykaeJ8Ak6HS49OLCLYoBASuNN4qvD4rYzAqtrdHVhjmDXPTWUJIYjT0x6UShYBasZy+CSvRbvEGFs/MXbKo8Sg6LTaju3E/QemqCHyl9ATivG9RvgkKec+c0MRpblI8SRBDxRlK/1KioEN5PuVdumizSo2Q8SRfkUZjG/uRN2G59A]
The Department has had months to prepare. They rejected repeated offers to meet with my office and with survivors’ attorneys to plan this release responsibly and transparently.
I recognize that there are millions of pages to review and that protecting victims’ identities and redacting graphic material is essential. Survivors must always come first.
But the north star remains justice for survivors, and for holding the rich and powerful men who raped young girls or covered up the abuse accountable.
The American people deserve a clear timeline for full compliance. DOJ must now tell the public when every unclassified record will be released and when the names of the powerful men who abused underage girls, or helped cover up the abuse, will finally be made public.
Add your name to demand action: The DOJ must today offer a clear timeline for the full release of the Epstein files. [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/epstein-files-release-timeline-petition?contactdata=5jrYr1tKAPGt0MbbSzoykaeJ8Ak6HS49OLCLYoBASuNN4qvD4rYzAqtrdHVhjmDXPTWUJIYjT0x6UShYBasZy+CSvRbvEGFs/MXbKo8Sg6LTaju3E/QemqCHyl9ATivG9RvgkKec+c0MRpblI8SRBDxRlK/1KioEN5PuVdumizSo2Q8SRfkUZjG/uRN2G59A]
SIGN THE PETITION [https://act.rokhanna.com/a/epstein-files-release-timeline-petition?contactdata=5jrYr1tKAPGt0MbbSzoykaeJ8Ak6HS49OLCLYoBASuNN4qvD4rYzAqtrdHVhjmDXPTWUJIYjT0x6UShYBasZy+CSvRbvEGFs/MXbKo8Sg6LTaju3E/QemqCHyl9ATivG9RvgkKec+c0MRpblI8SRBDxRlK/1KioEN5PuVdumizSo2Q8SRfkUZjG/uRN2G59A]
No one is above the law. Not even the Epstein class.
Thank you for standing up for transparency, accountability, and the rule of law.
In solidarity,
Ro Khanna
Wait! One more thing–
Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.
If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!
DONATE $3 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-footer-donate?refcode=20251219-epstein-release-timeline&amount=3]
DONATE MORE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-footer-donate?refcode=20251219-epstein-release-timeline]

Dear Friends and Partners,

As I reflect on the past year, I am filled with deep gratitude – for you, for your steadfast support, and for the courageous
mothers across our nation who are rising with conviction and an unshakable love for America.

2025 was a historic year for Moms for America. We expanded into new communities, defended parental rights with national leaders,
equipped thousands of moms through civics and constitutional training, launched Moms for Freedom World, and continued advancing
faith, family, and freedom wherever these values are challenged.

This impact came from moms – from kitchen tables and prayer circles to school board rooms and rallies across all 50 states. Your
partnership made this movement possible.

Because of you, we strengthened parental rights, challenged federal overreach, trained new leaders, added dozens of Moms for
America groups, and stood boldly for the Constitution at a critical moment in our nation’s history.

As we approach America’s 250th anniversary, our mission is clear: restore our culture, reclaim our history, and prepare the next
generation to carry the torch of liberty.

Please take a moment to look at our Year in Review of 2025 and celebrate all the highlights we achieved together to save America.

Together, we will restore this Republic,
one family at a time.

clean_body: Moms for America is so grateful for your continued support and confidence in us and our mighty movement of moms! If you are having trouble viewing this email click here to view in your browser. [] [] [] Dear Friends and Partners, As I reflect on the past year, I am filled with deep gratitude – for you, for your steadfast support, and for the courageous mothers across our nation who are rising with conviction and an unshakable love for America. 2025 was a historic year for Moms for America. We expanded into new communities, defended parental rights with national leaders, equipped thousands of moms through civics and constitutional training, launched Moms for Freedom World, and continued advancing faith, family, and freedom wherever these values are challenged. This impact came from moms – from kitchen tables and prayer circles to school board rooms and rallies across all 50 states. Your partnership made this movement possible. Because of you, we strengthened parental rights, challenged federal overreach, trained new leaders, added dozens of Moms for America groups, and stood boldly for the Constitution at a critical moment in our nation’s history. As we approach America’s 250th anniversary, our mission is clear: restore our culture, reclaim our history, and prepare the next generation to carry the torch of liberty. Please take a moment to look at our Year in Review of 2025 and celebrate all the highlights we achieved together to save America. Together, we will restore this Republic, one family at a time. 2025Year in Review [] View the Flip Book [] Download the PDF [] Please make your tax-deductible donation before midnight on December 31 and ensure we launch 2026 with the strength to protect our families and freedoms. DONATE NOW [] [] In Joy & Gratitude, Kimberly Fletcher Founder and President Moms for America® P.S. The clock is ticking. Midnight on December 31 is your last chance to make a tax-deductible donation for 2025. Stand with us – click here to give today. [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] Copyright © 2025 Moms for America, All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: Moms for America 1440 State HWY 248, Suite Q304 Branson, MO 65616 Visit our Website [] Visit our Store [] Visit us on Truth Social [] DONATE TODAY []

name: Roy Cooper
subject: Trump is taking the stage in North Carolina
body:
What Trump said:
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
Michael Whatley is bringing President Trump to my backyard today, Peter. He’ll be speaking later tonight. We’ve all seen what a Trump rally looks like. I don’t expect this one will be different. We know that wherever he goes, he takes aim at the Democratic leaders around him — he’s even attacked me personally before. **[My team expects Trump to send the MAGA donor machine after us again tonight. I need your help to take them on — can you pitch in and help us out?](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3159?t=7&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)** Peter, I know that when Trump’s name-calling starts, it’s because he’s terrified that the people of North Carolina will choose me over his hand-picked candidate. He knows this is the number one flip from red to blue Senate seat in the country and he wants to hold on to it with his yes man. Trump also knows that if you elect me to serve you instead of Michael Whatley, we can flip the U.S. Senate blue and stop his dangerous agenda in its tracks. **He knows I’ll never stop fighting for the priorities we share — like affordable health care, a planet where our children can live healthy lives, lower everyday costs, and our right to vote. He knows that our people-first movement can defeat him, so he’s doing everything possible to tear us down.** If that isn’t bad enough, we also learned this week that Elon Musk began to fund some Washington Republicans in their 2026 races. We shouldn’t have to worry about an unpredictable billionaire skewing our elections — but it’s yet another reason I need you with us now. **[Trump and Elon Musk are hoping that we will all forget how costly the MAGA agenda has been to folks here in North Carolina and across the country. We will not forget. We will fight back and get this country back on track. Our race is the most competitive Senate contest of 2026, and there’s no way I can win it alone. I’m humbly asking you to invest in our work, so we have the resources we need to take on the national GOP.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3159?t=8&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3160?t=9&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
---
[DONATE $10](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3161?t=10&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
---
[DONATE $25](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3162?t=11&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)

---
[DONATE $100](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3164?t=13&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3165?t=14&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
---
I’m grateful you’re with us,
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/3159?t=15&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?source=&t=4&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=16&akid=201%2E124072%2ELlipiQ)

name: Michael Bennet
subject: Will you commit to voting out the GOP?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
| | |
---
Team – Trump and Republicans' "big promised health care vote" to save Affordable Care Act tax credits came and went last week.
Yet nothing's changed about the fact that on Jan. 1, premiums will skyrocket for roughly 225,000 Coloradans and 24 million folks across the country – **because on Trump's orders, Republicans voted down our bill to extend the subsidies and stop that from happening.**
And after a year of helping Trump's agenda succeed, GOP Senate leadership has proven to be a **catastrophic failure** for working families.
_[If you agree, then click now to be one of the 15,000 folks I need to sign my emergency petition pledging to vote them out of power in the midterms before 11:59 p.m. tonight.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630313/590077487/436072886?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1022-su-nd&refcode=jtk1022-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODU3ZWQyZS0yZmRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WLTMGYLYmA1H2rlgbWvuRpUGxSNPJ73y2YOQzvHaQTo=&emci=c93a9693-8adb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3857ed2e-2fdd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=1295540)_
| | [PLEDGE TO VOTE →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630314/590077488/436072886?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1022-su-nd&refcode=jtk1022-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODU3ZWQyZS0yZmRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WLTMGYLYmA1H2rlgbWvuRpUGxSNPJ73y2YOQzvHaQTo=&emci=c93a9693-8adb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3857ed2e-2fdd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=1295540)
---
As long as Trump runs the White House and Republicans who refuse to defy him run the Senate, meaningful action to lower health care costs is nearly impossible. But Colorado can't afford to wait on Washington to come to grips with reality, let alone deliver the affordable health care solutions we need.
That's a big reason why I'm running for governor: **Because health care is a right.**
**And as Colorado's next governor, I'll do everything in my power to make it truly affordable, reliably accessible, and a right we enjoy universally.**
But until then, I'll be laser-focused on delivering a health care plan in the Senate that _actually works_ for working families. I just need your help to make it a reality – _[and team, it starts with you pledging to vote out the Republican Senate majority.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630315/590077489/436072886?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1022-su-nd&refcode=jtk1022-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzODU3ZWQyZS0yZmRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WLTMGYLYmA1H2rlgbWvuRpUGxSNPJ73y2YOQzvHaQTo=&emci=c93a9693-8adb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=3857ed2e-2fdd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=1295540)_
Thank you for fighting alongside me,

Michael
| Bennet for Governor
PO Box 270117
Louisville, CO 80027
United States **Paid for by Bennet for Governor. name: Brianna Shamsuddoha, NDRC
subject: How these puzzle pieces fit together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
We need grassroots support to keep pushing back on every crucial front
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630433/590077900/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQwMjU0YWNhLTJmZGQtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMGQzYTFlYzhjNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lxJWu2c_sbJkp64i6elht7NSyuHDC6xm9FoPfAK6X-c=&emci=4944a602-f5dc-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=40254aca-2fdd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, For almost a decade, the NDRC has been responding to all kinds of attacks on fair representation, from gerrymandering to anti-voter legislation. But in 2025, Donald Trump and Republicans launched a coordinated effort to dismantle the core tenets of our democracy, creating an unprecedented redistricting crisis. As we confront our new reality, I want to take a step back and talk to you about how we at NDRC envision our work — especially in times like these, when it feels like the odds are stacked against us. NDRC’s logo is a puzzle piece. We chose that image because it illustrates how the work we’re doing to rebuild a democracy where voters pick their politicians — not the other way around — is all connected. Peter, it’s all the same fight. The way I see it, the same Republicans fighting to gut the Voting Rights Act, disenfranchise Black voters, and exclude certain residents from the census are the same ones trying to rig the midterm elections, manipulate maps, and strip naturalized citizens of their right to vote. Protecting the voting rights of people of color is inextricably linked to passing and defending fair maps, ensuring an accurate census count, and protecting our most fundamental rights — from reproductive freedom to economic security and beyond. We’ve all seen it this year. Texas was the first state to fall in Trump’s redistricting crisis, creating a domino effect in states like Missouri, North Carolina, and more. And we know what happens in one state is often an indication of what’s to come across the rest of the country. **Our logo is a reminder that the fight for fair representation is made of many pieces and requires focusing both on the details of individual states and the greater threat to our entire nation. The NDRC is committed to doing the work. But that type of fight — especially during an unprecedented redistricting crisis — takes enormous resources.** [**That’s why I’m asking you to make a donation to help fuel the massive undertaking required to keep pushing back on all these fronts — from defending fair maps to protecting the voting power of marginalized communities.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630434/590077901/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_PuzzleMatters_email_20251219-NDRC-Tier1-PuzzleMatters_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251219-NDRC-Tier1-PuzzleMatters_&utm_campaign=PuzzleMatters&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQwMjU0YWNhLTJmZGQtZjAxMS04MTk0LTAwMGQzYTFlYzhjNiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=lxJWu2c_sbJkp64i6elht7NSyuHDC6xm9FoPfAK6X-c=) | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: We need grassroots support to keep pushing back on every crucial front | Peter, For almost a decade, the NDRC has been responding to all kinds of attacks on fair representation, from gerrymandering to anti-voter legislation. But in 2025, Donald Trump and Republicans launched a coordinated effort to dismantle the core tenets of our democracy, creating an unprecedented redistricting crisis. As we confront our new reality, I want to take a step back and talk to you about how we at NDRC envision our work — especially in times like these, when it feels like the odds are stacked against us. NDRC’s logo is a puzzle piece. We chose that image because it illustrates how the work we’re doing to rebuild a democracy where voters pick their politicians — not the other way around — is all connected. Peter, it’s all the same fight. The way I see it, the same Republicans fighting to gut the Voting Rights Act, disenfranchise Black voters, and exclude certain residents from the census are the same ones trying to rig the midterm elections, manipulate maps, and strip naturalized citizens of their right to vote. Protecting the voting rights of people of color is inextricably linked to passing and defending fair maps, ensuring an accurate census count, and protecting our most fundamental rights — from reproductive freedom to economic security and beyond. We’ve all seen it this year. Texas was the first state to fall in Trump’s redistricting crisis, creating a domino effect in states like Missouri, North Carolina, and more. And we know what happens in one state is often an indication of what’s to come across the rest of the country. Our logo is a reminder that the fight for fair representation is made of many pieces and requires focusing both on the details of individual states and the greater threat to our entire nation. The NDRC is committed to doing the work. But that type of fight — especially during an unprecedented redistricting crisis — takes enormous resources. = | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you, Brianna Shamsuddoha Mobilization Director, NDRC = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NW, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. _[If you were forwarded this newsletter, you can sign up to receive the Kernel every week by clicking here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630564/590078484/1416943042?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2Y0NjViMS0zMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1yR1TUqfRh67uAocZWOCZMSldo5M5OSSxaZ5B6E-67k=&emci=07263348-11dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=27f465b1-30dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4925535)_ [_**If you'd like to donate to the INDems' Reorganization Fund, you can do so by clicking here.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630565/590078491/1764064610?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2Y0NjViMS0zMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1yR1TUqfRh67uAocZWOCZMSldo5M5OSSxaZ5B6E-67k=) ****
_Every Indiana Republican Blocks Bill to Lower Healthcare Premiums for Hoosiers_ [__](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630566/590078495/86523165?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2Y0NjViMS0zMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1yR1TUqfRh67uAocZWOCZMSldo5M5OSSxaZ5B6E-67k=&emci=07263348-11dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=27f465b1-30dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4925535) On Wednesday, the U.S. House of Representatives rejected a Democratic-led proposal to extend healthcare premium tax credits into 2026. Republicans got rid of these tax credits with the passage of their Big Bad Bill earlier this year, and hundreds of thousands of Hoosiers are already seeing their rates increase by thousands of dollars in 2026. The bill failed by just a margin of one vote, 204-203, meaning that a single vote by any of the seven Indiana Republicans in Congress could've passed the tax credits. It seems as if Reps. Yakym, Stutzman, Baird, Spartz, Shreve, Messmer, and Houchin are more than happy to see Hoosiers charged more for healthcare, even if it means thousands of their own constituents lose their coverage. ****_Indiana Named as Having the Nation's Worst Roads_
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630567/590078509/-1219507624?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2Y0NjViMS0zMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1yR1TUqfRh67uAocZWOCZMSldo5M5OSSxaZ5B6E-67k=&emci=07263348-11dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=27f465b1-30dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4925535) A new survey was released this week of truck drivers from across the country, and it found that Indiana has the worst road conditions in the entire United States. Too many throughfares and state highways in Indiana are in poor shape across our state, and this survey is proof of what Hoosiers are feeling on the roads. This survey asked those who drive for a living to chime in, and Indiana's standing should give our Republican state government pause. What is their plan to change our state's repuation and making driving safer for the millions of Hoosiers who must use our roads every day? It's time to elect new leadership that doesn't neglegt our infrastructure like the Braun-Beckwith Republican supermajority does. ****_Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks._ [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121630568/590078514/-679233957?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyN2Y0NjViMS0zMGRkLWYwMTEtODE5NC0wMDBkM2ExZWM4YzYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1yR1TUqfRh67uAocZWOCZMSldo5M5OSSxaZ5B6E-67k=&emci=07263348-11dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&emdi=27f465b1-30dd-f011-8194-000d3a1ec8c6&ceid=4925535) U.S. Rep. Frank Mrvan this week celebrated the awarding of federal dollars to revitalize the Little Calumet River in Gary. This money comes from the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation’s (NFWF) National Coastal Resilience Fund, which helps coastal communities protect their wetlands and other vital resources for future generations. Projects like this help turn more of Northwest Indiana into livable, vibrant green spaces that the whole community can enjoy. Rep. clean_body: | Welcome to the Indiana Democratic Party's weekly newsletter, The Kernel of Truth. The State Party will provide the most important facts - a kernel of truth - from the week. = ** Every Indiana Republican Blocks Bill to Lower Healthcare Premiums for Hoosiers On Wednesday, the U.S. House of Representatives rejected a Democratic-led proposal to extend healthcare premium tax credits into 2026. Republicans got rid of these tax credits with the passage of their Big Bad Bill earlier this year, and hundreds of thousands of Hoosiers are already seeing their rates increase by thousands of dollars in 2026. The bill failed by just a margin of one vote, 204-203, meaning that a single vote by any of the seven Indiana Republicans in Congress could've passed the tax credits. It seems as if Reps. Yakym, Stutzman, Baird, Spartz, Shreve, Messmer, and Houchin are more than happy to see Hoosiers charged more for healthcare, even if it means thousands of their own constituents lose their coverage. Indiana Named as Having the Nation's Worst Roads A new survey was released this week of truck drivers from across the country, and it found that Indiana has the worst road conditions in the entire United States. Too many throughfares and state highways in Indiana are in poor shape across our state, and this survey is proof of what Hoosiers are feeling on the roads. This survey asked those who drive for a living to chime in, and Indiana's standing should give our Republican state government pause. What is their plan to change our state's repuation and making driving safer for the millions of Hoosiers who must use our roads every day? It's time to elect new leadership that doesn't neglegt our infrastructure like the Braun-Beckwith Republican supermajority does. Each week, the State Party will highlight key policy from Democrats at the Statehouse or across the state. Democrats deliver a better future for Hoosier families, and we urge you to share this information with your networks. U.S. Rep. Frank Mrvan this week celebrated the awarding of federal dollars to revitalize the Little Calumet River in Gary. This money comes from the National Fish and Wildlife Foundation’s (NFWF) National Coastal Resilience Fund, which helps coastal communities protect their wetlands and other vital resources for future generations. Projects like this help turn more of Northwest Indiana into livable, vibrant green spaces that the whole community can enjoy. Rep. Mrvan will never stop fighting to protect the critical water resources that the Region, and all of Indiana, depend on. Information on County Party events, Town Halls, protests, volunteer opportunities, and more can be found on our Upcoming Candidate Trainings Join the National Democratic Training Committee on Thursday, January 8th from 1-2 pm ET for a free virtual live training on So You Want to Run for Office. INDems IN the News IndyStar: WTHR: Indiana Capital Chronicle: * Indiana Capital Chronicle: [content goes here] 101 W Washington St., #1110E Indianapolis, IN 46204 Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Brandon Herrera
subject: LAST DAY: Win the AKG Occam Warfighter + BONUS
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Brandon Herrera Victory Committee
body:
Patriot,
Election Day is just 74 days away, and while our campaign is way ahead of where we were last time, we are still far behind my opponent in a vital metric: cash on hand.
You can help us close the gap by making a contribution before the end of the year at [https://BrandonHerreraforCongress.com/Donate](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/l/6fI53a1vdu7637OWRF387YCA/z0FRm1I0XrBWoiinf9nfZQ/Vl5sP6h6O25fLCM6892pPv892g).
Today is also the last day to earn a chance to win the AKG Occam Warfighter rifle that I made in my shop in San Antonio (photo below). Every $5 you donate earns you one chance to win, so $100 gets you twenty entries.
I also have a special bonus: **if you donate today, you will earn chances to win our next giveaway, which may be our biggest prize yet.** I can't announce it yet because we are still working out a few details, but I can assure you it will be something the winner will never forget.
So not only do your donations today earn you a chance to win the Warfighter, they also count toward winning the next giveaway.
Please make your donation today at [https://BrandonHerreraforCongress.com/Donate](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/l/6fI53a1vdu7637OWRF387YCA/uHqYil2KAgObPCL1BNcVjw/Vl5sP6h6O25fLCM6892pPv892g).
Victory is only possible because of your help, and I will always be grateful.
Respectfully,

Brandon Herrera
PS: Today is the last day to earn a chance to win the Occam Warfighter, but your donations today and today only will also earn you entries into our next giveaway, which may be our biggest ever. Do not miss out. Contribute at [https://BrandonHerreraforCongress.com/Donate](https://contact.brandonherreraforcongress.com/l/6fI53a1vdu7637OWRF387YCA/RZkgdhqXj3sgdI892MPGNCVA/Vl5sP6h6O25fLCM6892pPv892g) today.

body:
| | | Co-Pilot, My Finance Director just gave me some bad news. Our end-of-year deadline is just over a week away, and we still need to raise _**$15,000**_ to meet our goal. _**This race isn’t just about saving Michigan. It's about saving America.**_ A RED Michigan means we will get a Republican in the White House in 2028. Michigan has helped decide presidential elections, with the most recent coming down to my state. **If we don’t meet this goal, our hopes of a Republican in the White House in 2028 could vanish.** I’m a proud Black conservative, a faithful Christian, and a combat veteran. My very existence flies in the face of the Left’s woke lies. _Now I need your help to fight back before the end-of-year deadline hits._ [**Pitch in NOW to save Michigan and America from the Radical Left!**](http://links.jjformichigan.com/u/click?_t=70fde66bc0554ca7a14c16f5ebafdbd9&_m=d0ce33fa09c646e8a24dcefb7f49080d&_e=mmFX1xfoTrzcDaa8doB7lReeiT0Ilqo0P4y1oELYaImWfmJpreOaHlBxQhTX_huJE5kPdK93XBCSCvCC8EntBENeMLOzBLJ_IpZp1vXaqZOjjGOsP04V7vpQ_YoVswUGP-ODNGfKenF0YQPhACaJ-mVuWm9YVNVuEzHqjiDiwx9wUcnmDyKl_YENOc_JZbqyeJVjJClzzTQZHfdVTX9_hJx3VL4ZNLknmkz4mLYZ6-Q94d5QIMsr3mcWzY5tOoRj8lwgKUYci3LK8hIb5OBmog7oi4q7S1sVU4i6H9eSATpNQSmM9M4uNbRSaVHTibbCWDQHQAIgfJhsX9KiPbSrCOAq0icw1ytKXP56vrb_FVtXkFXosPo_evV7pUgJpBI0ULri2EiGCHuDeFvvEHp4INRLNGv2oaqUv7kPamrwRq4%3D)

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/30aaf7de-aa6a-4302-ae3e-2fb321b8becf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* * *
In this week's _Blue Amp Media_ update, David Shuster takes viewers on a brisk but bracing tour through a series of moments that, taken together, paint a troubling picture of where American governance--and basic competence--stand right now. It's one of those episodes where each story on its own would be bad enough, but stacked together they feel like something bigger is breaking.
Shuster opens on Capitol Hill, where House Republican leadership is once again trying to block a vote that a majority of Americans support. The issue at hand: extending Affordable Care Act subsidies that millions rely on. What makes this moment different is the fracture inside the GOP itself. A small group of Republicans defied both Donald Trump and Speaker Mike Johnson, signing onto a Democratic-led maneuver that forces the issue into the open. Shuster explains why this isn't just inside-baseball politics--it's a crack in the façade of party discipline, and it has real consequences for people's lives.
* * *
Get more Shuster in your inbox! Subscribe for free.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.z71t9dB-y1dhuBI1ljD-N-kGV02OVx4FaFtiZhYh658?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=182128172)
* * *
From there, the focus shifts to the Senate and a seemingly simple question that spirals into something far more revealing: is the FCC an independent agency or not? What follows is a stunning example of institutional evasiveness, capped by a quiet but telling change to official language after the fact. It's a segment that underscores how power now often works--not by arguing facts, but by obscuring them.
The episode then turns darker, as Shuster walks through conflicting claims from the Department of Defense and what actually happened during a near-disastrous military aviation incident. A commercial flight, a U.S. Air Force refueler, and a transponder that wasn't turned on--details that raise serious questions about safety, accountability, and truth-telling at the highest levels.
And looming over all of it is Donald Trump himself. Shuster revisits recent remarks and behavior that go well beyond exaggeration or bravado, asking viewers to consider what we're really watching unfold in real time. It's not mockery for its own sake--it's concern, grounded in facts and context.
As always, Shuster balances the gravity with sharp clarity--and even a bit of dark humor--before closing with a reminder that staying informed, engaged, and yes, even laughing when appropriate, is part of staying sane in an upside-down moment.
* * *
